data.text: I’m checking the existing agent loop and stop/retry code first so I can decide whether this bead is small enough to execute or needs a split. If it’s still too broad at this depth cap, I’ll stop with a no-changes rationale rather than half-implementing it.

FEAT-010 names DDx-owned packages/roles using task execution vocabulary (`run`, `try`, `work`, `taskexec`, `fizeauadapter`) rather than `internal/agent`.\n4. ADR-024 says escalation changes only MinPower/MaxPower/request facts and Fizeau chooses the concrete route.\n5. TP-020 is replaced or superseded with a DDx boundary test plan proving DDx does not normalize, fuzzy-match, or route models.\n6. docs/migrations/routing-config.md documents removal of `agent.*` defaults and the split between Fizeau routing config and DDx execution config.\n7. DDx skill references explain explicit passthrough and Fizeau-owned model matching using examples such as `--model qwen36`, while stating DDx passes the raw string unchanged.\n8. `rg -n \"ddx agent|ddx-agent|agentlib.DdxAgent|Agent Service|cli/internal/agent\" docs .agents/skills .claude/skills skills` returns only audited historical or literal-external-agent hits documented in the change.\n9. `ddx doc validate` passes or any unrelated pre-existing failures are listed in the bead notes.\n10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.",
.ddx/executions/20260506T174329-7cffbd58/prompt.md:11:1. FEAT-001 removes the `ddx agent` structural mount and defines only `ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx work`, and any Fizeau diagnostics namespace that remains, with no aliases.
2. FEAT-006 is renamed or rewritten as the DDx consumer spec for Fizeau, and no longer presents `agentlib.DdxAgent` / `ddx-agent` as the desired contract name.
3. FEAT-010 names DDx-owned packages/roles using task execution vocabulary (`run`, `try`, `work`, `taskexec`, `fizeauadapter`) rather than `internal/agent`.
4. ADR-024 says escalation changes only MinPower/MaxPower/request facts and Fizeau chooses the concrete route.
5. TP-020 is replaced or superseded with a DDx boundary test plan proving DDx does not normalize, fuzzy-match, or route models.
6. docs/migrations/routing-config.md documents removal of `agent.*` defaults and the split between Fizeau routing config and DDx execution config.
7. DDx skill references explain explicit passthrough and Fizeau-owned model matching using examples such as `--model qwen36`, while stating DDx passes the raw string unchanged.
8. `rg -n "ddx agent|ddx-agent|agentlib.DdxAgent|Agent Service|cli/internal/agent" docs .agents/skills .claude/skills skills` returns only audited historical or literal-external-agent hits documented in the change.
9. `ddx doc validate` passes or any unrelated pre-existing failures are listed in the bead notes.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

3. FEAT-010 names DDx-owned packages/roles using task execution vocabulary (`run`, `try`, `work`, `taskexec`, `fizeauadapter`) rather than `internal/agent`.
4. ADR-024 says escalation changes only MinPower/MaxPower/request facts and Fizeau chooses the concrete route.
5. TP-020 is replaced or superseded with a DDx boundary test plan proving DDx does not normalize, fuzzy-match, or route models.
6. docs/migrations/routing-config.md documents removal of `agent.*` defaults and the split between Fizeau routing config and DDx execution config.
7. DDx skill references explain explicit passthrough and Fizeau-owned model matching using examples such as `--model qwen36`, while stating DDx passes the raw string unchanged.
8. `rg -n "ddx agent|ddx-agent|agentlib.DdxAgent|Agent Service|cli/internal/agent" docs .agents/skills .claude/skills skills` returns only audited historical or literal-external-agent hits documented in the change.
9. `ddx doc validate` passes or any unrelated pre-existing failures are listed in the bead notes.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

.ddx/executions/20260506T172809-7a0a7018/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
ddx work currently continues scanning and claiming ready beads after setup/infrastructure failures. During the May 6 ENOSPC incident, ddx work failed to create one isolated worktree, then immediately claimed another production-reachability bead and failed again. Resource exhaustion must stop the drain because continuing expands the blast radius.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 initializes loop events and exitReason but does not include resource_exhausted as a recognized loop-fatal exit reason.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:529 onward continues iterating after non-success reports unless existing retry/no-progress/once logic stops it; there is no host-resource circuit breaker.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:263-283 defines status taxonomy around execution_failed/no_evidence/etc., but there is no distinct resource_exhausted status/reason for loop policy to inspect.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:434-440 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:281-284 require ddx work to stop claiming new beads after resource_exhausted.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a stable resource_exhausted attempt/loop classification that can be returned by ddx try/resource preflight and recognized by ExecuteBeadWorker.
- On resource_exhausted, release any non-terminal claim, emit cleanup/resource events, set loop.end exit_reason=resource_exhausted, and return without scanning the next ready bead.
- Print an operator-visible message such as resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop.
- Ensure resource_exhausted is not counted as model no-progress, capability retry, or review/quota retry.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the resource checker itself here; this bead consumes the outcome from the try/resource-preflight bead.
- Do not change queue ranking or production-reachability bead priority.
- Do not change provider 429 in-attempt retry behavior.
.ddx/executions/20260506T172809-7a0a7018/prompt.md:11:1. TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedStopsLoop creates two ready beads and an executor returning resource_exhausted for the first; verifies the second bead is never claimed or executed.
2. TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedUnclaimsAndNoCooldown verifies any claimed bead is open/re-claimable and no execute-loop-retry-after/no-progress cooldown is written.
3. TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedLoopEndEvent verifies loop.end includes exit_reason=resource_exhausted and cleanup/resource detail is emitted.
4. TestWorkResourceExhausted_PrintsOperatorMessage verifies the ddx work command output includes resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop or the finalized exact message.
5. Resource exhaustion remains distinct from review transport/quota and provider rate-limit retry in status/detail handling.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run "TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhausted|TestWorkResourceExhausted" -count=1 passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

.ddx/executions/20260506T163511-7a594d13/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
After the root context is cancelled by Ctrl-C/SIGTERM, `ddx work` and `ddx try` must finish the TD-031 interruption path: preserve available evidence, classify the attempt as mechanically disrupted, release any non-terminal bead claim, remove runtime liveness state, and leave the unfinished bead re-claimable. The current code has partial context-cancellation classification but needs explicit end-to-end coverage for the CLI work/try paths.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:859-878 marks non-success reports as disrupted when ctx is cancelled, but the surrounding cleanup/unclaim behavior is only incidentally covered by generic failure branches.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:812-846 starts and stops claim heartbeat around an attempt; interrupted attempts must stop liveness and release or clear claims deterministically.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:797-807 creates a dispatch context and only the bead-extra cancel path forces `operator_cancel`; plain context cancellation should remain `signal`/mechanical disruption, not `operator_cancel`.
- cli/cmd/try.go:333-342 and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1921-1940 propagate `cmd.Context()` into worker.Run but need regression tests that cancellation leaves the target bead re-claimable and reports the signal path.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:289-314 defines the claim release and no-cooldown requirements for worker shutdown/interruption.

PROPOSED FIX
- Make interrupted attempts produce a stable disrupted/interrupted report reason (for example `context_canceled` / `signal`) without counting as model no-progress.
- Ensure the loop unclaims interrupted non-terminal beads and removes any external claim heartbeat/liveness record.
- Ensure interrupted attempts do not set `execute-loop-retry-after` unless a separate retryable condition was already recorded before cancellation.
- Ensure `ddx try <id>` reports a clear interrupted outcome and exits non-zero after cleanup.
- Add regression tests around worker.Run plus command-level try/work cancellation paths using controllable fake executors/runners.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not preserve worktrees as resumable by default. Cleanup/retry-from-scratch is the reliable default.
- Do not alter operator cancel-marker semantics (`cancel-requested` / `operator_cancel`).
- Do not change successful landing/review behavior when cancellation arrives after a terminal mutation.
.ddx/executions/20260506T163511-7a594d13/prompt.md:11:1. TestWorkInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsBead verifies cancelling the work command context during an in-flight attempt releases the claim and leaves the bead open/re-claimable.
2. TestWorkInterrupt_DoesNotSetNoProgressCooldown verifies interrupted work does not set `execute-loop-retry-after` or increment no-progress suppression.
3. TestWorkInterrupt_RemovesClaimLiveness verifies external claim heartbeat/liveness is removed or stale-safe after cleanup.
4. TestTryInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsTarget verifies `ddx try <id>` cancellation leaves the target bead re-claimable and exits non-zero with an interrupted/signal-class outcome.
5. TestTryInterrupt_DoesNotReportOperatorCancel verifies plain context cancellation is not classified as `operator_cancel` unless the bead cancel marker was present.
6. TestInterruptedAfterTerminalMutation_DoesNotUndoClose verifies cancellation after a successful terminal mutation does not reopen or unclaim a closed bead.
7. `rg -n "context.Canceled|context_canceled|signal|operator_cancel|execute-loop-retry-after" cli/internal/agent cli/cmd` shows cancellation paths are explicit and comments distinguish signal interruption from operator cancel.
8. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run "TestWorkInterrupt|TestTryInterrupt|TestInterrupted|TestCancel|TestClaim" -count=1` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-fdd3ea36/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes\nrationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)\n\n## Decision\n\nStopping without a commit. This bead's acceptance criteria describe a\nmulti-PR release-adoption epic, not a single bounded change. Attempting it\nin one execute-bead pass would produce either (a) a partial commit that\nfails most AC items, or (b) a sprawling change touching dispatch, config\nschema, CLI surface, frontend Playwright fixtures, and CHANGELOG/docs\nwithout the up-front design alignment the bead itself calls for.\n\n## What is already done (verified in this worktree)\n\n1. **Upstream routing-plan beads are all closed.** All six beads under\n   label `routing-plan` in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` are in\n   `closed` status:\n     - agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware routing)\n     - agent-191a74f9 (default + local + standard profiles)\n     - agent-1f46cf22 (routing docs)\n     - agent-53f38d95 (public decision trace)\n     - agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)\n     - agent-dfabb10b (typed errors)\n\n2. **Their closing commits are in DDx's currently-pinned upstream tag.**\n   `cli/go.mod` references `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.\n   `git tag --contains` for each routing-plan closing commit returns\n   v0.9.10 as the earliest containing tag. v0.9.23 (and v0.9.24 latest)\n   transitively contain all of them. So the *binary surface* AC #1\n   wants is already accessible via the pinned go.mod.\n\n## What is NOT done — and why this can't be a one-shot pass\n\n### AC #1 — CHANGELOG-as-released-narrative\n\nAC #1 asks for \"a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class\ndefault and local profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs +\ngating; ErrHarnessModelIncompatible; public decision-trace …; cost\ndimension on ProviderEntry with unknown-cost fallback.\"\n\nReading `~/Projects/agent/CHANGELOG.md`:\n  - v0.9.10 documents prompt-caching work, not the routing-plan work.\n  - v0.9.11 documents ADR-006 override-as-failure-signal.\n  - v0.9.12-v0.9.24 cover lucebox/vllm, conformance, corpus, terminal-bench,\n    pi-runner panic. None of these releases bundles a coherent\n    \"routing point release\" narrative.\n\nThe closing commits for the six beads landed scattered between tags as\nside-quest work; none of those tags' release notes call out the\ncombined contract DDx now wants to consume. AC #1 effectively requires\neither (a) an upstream re-release that publishes a \"v0.9.25 — routing\npoint release\" entry covering exactly those items, OR (b) DDx's bead\ndescription must be relaxed to accept \"the code is in v0.9.23, even if\nno single CHANGELOG block summarizes it.\" Either resolution is a\ngovernance decision, not a coding task this worktree can execute.\n\n### AC #2-7 — DDx-side surface (D1-D5)\n\nThese criteria collectively require:\n  - rewriting the worker dispatch path so the default issues exactly one\n    `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")` call with no Model / no Harness /\n    no Provider, removing all DDx-side ladder synthesis;\n  - inserting a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before bead claim\n    (`execute_bead_loop.go` ~line 341);\n  - making `agent.routing.default_harness` a hard error at config load,\n    `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` opt-in via `--escalate`/`--override-model`,\n    with one-time process warnings;\n  - guaranteeing override flag combinations route through unchanged to\n    upstream and surface upstream typed errors;\n  - removing `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef` /\n    `workersByHarness` escalation helpers from the default path with\n    dead-code detection in CI;\n  - adding an integration test that reproduces the historical 19-burn\n    drain-queue bug AND new e2e tests covering each override\n    combination (claude+opus, model-only, harness-only, profile+harness\n    conflict);\n  - adding Playwright drain-queue golden fixtures matching the user's\n    real config;\n  - updating CHANGELOG and docs.\n\nThis is on the order of 1-3k LOC delta across:\n  - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go`\n  - `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` (workerDispatchAdapter)\n  - `cli/internal/config/*` (deprecated field schema)\n  - `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` (override flag plumbing)\n  - new tests (unit, integration, regression, Playwright)\n\nThat work spans D1-D5 in the bead's own sequencing list, which the bead\nexplicitly bundles into \"one DDx PR\" — but a PR of that size needs\narchitectural alignment with the user (especially around the breaking\nconfig change in D4 and the dead-code detector in AC #7) before the\nfirst line is written. execute-bead's contract is to satisfy the AC\ndeterministically, not to make architecture decisions.\n\n### AC #8 — D6 / D7 follow-up beads\n\nThe bead requires that follow-up beads be filed:\n  - one for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer,\n  - acknowledgement that `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workersByProject filter)\n    stays independent.\n\nFiling beads requires `ddx bead create` invocations against the\nproject's tracker and a commit that mutates `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. This is\nmechanical but should ride along with the substantive PR, not a no-op.\n\n### AC #10 — drain-queue regression golden\n\nA net-new integration test that reproduces the user's historical\n19-burn failure with their real config. Designing this test\n(particularly the gemini-binary-installed assertion, since CI may not\nhave gemini available) is itself a sub-bead.\n\n## What a follow-up attempt needs\n\nTo make this bead landable in a single execute-bead pass, the bead\nmust be split into smaller beads, each of which is a well-defined PR:\n\n1. **routing-bump** — bump `cli/go.mod` from v0.9.23 to whatever\n   tag (v0.9.25?) the upstream cuts as the routing point release; add\n   DDx CHANGELOG entry citing upstream release notes. Trivially\n   satisfies AC #1 once upstream cuts the tag.\n\n2. **routing-default-path** — D1 + D2 only. Worker dispatch + default\n   `ddx agent run` and `ddx work` issue `ResolveRoute(Profile:\n   \"default\")`. New unit tests. Does not touch config schema yet.\n\n3. **routing-preflight-gate** — D3 only. Insert preflight before\n   `Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~341`. Test with seeded\n   incompatibility.\n\n4. **routing-config-deprecation** — D4 only. Hard error on\n   `default_harness`, opt-in warnings on others. Migration test +\n   CHANGELOG breaking-change note.\n\n5. **routing-overrides-e2e** — D5 + AC #5 e2e tests for each override\n   combination.\n\n6. **routing-drain-regression** — AC #10. Standalone regression test\n   that reproduces the historical 19-burn bug.\n\n7. **routing-cleanup** — AC #7 dead-code detector + removal of\n   compensating logic.\n\n8. **routing-frontend-goldens** — AC #6 Playwright fixtures.\n\n9. **routing-followups** — AC #8 file the route-status / providers-UI\n   bead.\n\nEach of those is a clean execute-bead pass with provable AC. The\ncurrent bead's AC list is the union of all nine — too broad for one\nattempt.\n\n## Recommendation to the orchestrator\n\nReopen this bead with `helix-evolve` (or equivalent) to split it into\nthe nine sub-beads above, then queue them in order. The first three\n(bump + default-path + preflight-gate) unblock the user's stated\ngoals (G1/G2/G3) in the smallest possible PR.\n\nThe reduced-scope language in the bead's own description (\"DDx-side\ntracker only\", \"blocks on a single upstream point release\") suggests\nthis bead was meant as the EPIC, not the deliverable. Treating it as\nthe deliverable in execute-bead is what produced this no-op outcome.\nresult_rev=891112dee30444c8925bbc101abd3b1da50a3441\nbase_rev=891112dee30444c8925bbc101abd3b1da50a3441\nretry_after=2026-04-29T04:30:22Z","created_at":"2026-04-28T22:30:22.575970819Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"no_changes"}

The recent bounced workflow beads showed the gap: the implementer correctly reported that the bead was too large, but decomposition was prohibited inside the attempted worktree/depth context, so an operator had to split it manually.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1386 embeds decomposition instructions in the implementer prompt, which happens after dispatch rather than before claim.\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1661 emits an implementation-attempt decomposition depth cap message; that prompt cap can prevent the worker from creating children even when the queue-level orchestrator could still split safely.\n- docs/triage/decomposition.md requires ac_map and parent blocking semantics, but execute-loop does not yet file children from a pre-claim intake decision or from post-attempt no_changes_needs_investigation caused by oversized work.\n- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:29 defines no_changes_needs_investigation, but the work loop does not yet convert a too-large rationale into orchestrator-level decomposition.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Handle pre-claim too_large_decomposed by creating child beads with standalone descriptions/AC and dependency wiring.\n- Handle post-attempt no_changes_needs_investigation whose rationale says the bead is too large, too broad, depth-capped inside the attempt, or requires decomposition by invoking the same orchestrator splitter on the original bead.\n- Treat implementation-attempt depth as advisory only; queue-level max_decomposition_depth is the limit that decides whether DDx can split or must ask the operator.\n- Parent receives triage-decomposed event body with child_ids, rationale, and ac_map.\n- Every parent AC must map to at least one child AC or explicit needs_human/non_scope; lossy decomposition blocks for operator instead of creating children.\n- Parent is blocked/dependency-blocked on children and not claimed again for implementation.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Safe rewrite handling.\n- Review-time decomposition beyond using the same splitter when review_too_large is already classified.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"codex\",\"resolved_model\":\"gpt-5.4-mini\",\"fallback_chain\":[],\"actual_power\":8}","created_at":"2026-05-06T07:14:46.730021942Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"attempt_id\":\"20260506T071425-bd3e30f7\",\"harness\":\"codex\",\"model\":\"gpt-5.4-mini\",\"input_tokens\":59940,\"output_tokens\":862,\"total_tokens\":60802,\"cost_usd\":0,\"duration_ms\":17777,\"exit_code\":0}","created_at":"2026-05-06T07:14:46.947554809Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=60802 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"The bead is still too large to execute safely in a single pass, but this worktree is already at decomposition depth 3. New cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go defines ReviewVerdict struct with Verdict (string, enum-validated), Findings ([]Finding), Summary (string), and SchemaVersion (int) fields.\n2. ParseReviewVerdict accepts: raw JSON; ```json …``` fenced JSON; mixed prose+JSON where the JSON is the last fenced block. Rejects: unknown verdict values; missing verdict field; truncated JSON.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestParseReviewVerdict ./internal/agent/... passes with one subtest per accepted/rejected case above.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerEmitsJSONContract ./internal/agent/... passes: uses a fixture reviewer transcript with '### Verdict: APPROVE' wrapped markdown and asserts the extractor call site rejects it with the 'unparseable' class — confirming the markdown path is gone.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerJSONHappyPath ./internal/agent/... passes: a fixture transcript containing the JSON contract parses to a ReviewVerdict with verdict=APPROVE.\n6. The reviewer prompt template (in execute_bead_review.go or its asset file) requests JSON-only output and includes a schema example; cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewPromptRequestsJSON ./internal/agent/... passes by string-matching the template.\n7. The previously-observed regression — extractor parsing 'BLOCK' from the options header line — has a regression test: cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerNoOptionsHeaderRegression ./internal/agent/... feeds a transcript that includes only the prompt template's options header echoed back and asserts the parser rejects it (unparseable), not parses it as BLOCK.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-25T09:24:10Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"196235","closing_commit_sha":"ed2219bf951ce9b7770ed684fb6c789a51b3344d","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:00:51.532484284Z","description":"Upstream report (\"ddx execute-loop: post-merge reviewer silently fails on large prompts\", suggested fix #5) flags that even when the reviewer returns content, the markdown verdict extractor mis-parses it: when Claude responds with '### Verdict: APPROVE' the extractor pulls 'BLOCK' from the options header line in the prompt template that the model echoes back, logging '{\"verdict\":\"BLOCK\"}' for an actual APPROVE. This silently lets regressions land.\n\nFEAT-022 (parent ddx-0c35470e) is scoped to prompt assembly and size invariants; response parsing is out of scope there. File this as a sibling.\n\nSwitch the reviewer contract to a machine-readable JSON object emitted as the model's final response:\n\n  {\n    \"verdict\": \"APPROVE\" | \"REQUEST_CHANGES\" | \"BLOCK\",\n    \"findings\": [\n      { \"severity\": \"info\"|\"warn\"|\"block\", \"summary\": \"…\", \"location\": \"path:line?\" }\n    ],\n    \"summary\": \"≤300 char human-readable verdict justification\"\n  }\n\nReplace the existing markdown extractor with a strict JSON parser:\n1. Update the reviewer prompt template (post-collapse: agent.BuildReviewPrompt) to instruct the model to emit ONLY a JSON object matching the schema, fenced as ```json … ``` for robustness against models that won't emit raw JSON.\n2. Add internal/agent/review_verdict.go with a typed ReviewVerdict struct and a ParseReviewVerdict([]byte) (ReviewVerdict, error) that strips an optional ```json fence and decodes; reject on unknown verdict values.\n3. Replace the regex-based extractor call site in execute_bead_review.go with ParseReviewVerdict; on parse error, emit the existing 'unparseable' review-error class (introduced by ddx-70c1d2e2) — do NOT close the bead.\n4. Schema-version the contract: include schema_version: 1 in the prompt; the parser tolerates unknown fields but rejects unknown verdict values.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (prompt template, extractor call site)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go (new)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict_test.go (new)\n- prompt template asset under cli/internal/agent/assets/ if separated\n\nOut of scope:\n- Bounded retry / error-class taxonomy (ddx-70c1d2e2 owns this; this bead emits 'unparseable' into that taxonomy)\n- Prompt size invariants (FEAT-022)\n- Grading verdicts (separate bead if needed; this is review-only)\n\nCross-reference: This bead's `unparseable` emission feeds into ddx-70c1d2e2's bounded-retry logic. ddx-70c1d2e2 should be reviewed once both land to confirm the unparseable retry budget is sane (the upstream report observed real APPROVE verdicts being lost, so unparseable should retry, not terminal-escalate immediately).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:39:10.457997834Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"attempt_id\":\"20260425T092410-e9991cad\",\"harness\":\"claude\",\"input_tokens\":56,\"output_tokens\":43684,\"total_tokens\":43740,\"cost_usd\":4.689253000000002,\"duration_ms\":899008,\"exit_code\":0}","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:39:10.52615806Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=43740 cost_usd=4.6893"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"escalation_count\":0,\"fallback_chain\":[],\"final_tier\":\"\",\"requested_profile\":\"\",\"requested_tier\":\"\",\"resolved_model\":\"\",\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"resolved_tier\":\"\"}","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:39:13.380291229Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"APPROVE\nClean implementation: typed ReviewVerdict struct, strict ParseReviewVerdict with sentinel ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable, prompt template rewritten to request JSON-only output (with \"must NOT still request `### AC Grades`\" assertion guarding regression). Reviewed and approved by user before any implementation beads are filed from the roadmap.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:14:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4025192","closing_commit_sha":"fabf513096b522e921020e323ef1bf4a9cf36afa","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:43:27.404796076Z","description":"Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).\n\nPROPOSED ARCHITECTURAL SHIFT\nWORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API. --local mode collapses to \"worker registers with itself\" using an in-process implementation of the same API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.\n\nTHE ADR EVALUATES AND CHOOSES AMONG\n\nPROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients\n- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)\n- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)\n- Worker polls /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server\n- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/event {kind, body}\n- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/result {bead-id, status, evidence}\n- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters \"disconnected\" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return\n- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead\n\nPROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous\n- Worker runs independently; no server required\n- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort\n- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator\n- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)\n- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel\n\nPROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present\n- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup\n- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue\n- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store\n- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)\n\nUNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)\n\nPOST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }\nGET  /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/disconnect → 204 (graceful)\n\nThe existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.\n\nOUTSTANDING BEADS THIS ADR MUST RECONCILE (overlap analysis required in the ADR output)\n\nABSORBED (implementation subsumed by the new architecture; ADR explicitly closes these as superseded once roadmap lands):\n- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): becomes the API request/response payload — the spec IS the wire format. Refactor concern dissolves.\n- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): ALREADY FIXED at commit 41cb762e via flat 30s poll-interval default + attempted-map reset. Whether the new architecture preserves these tactical fixes or replaces them via API-driven semantics needs an ADR decision.\n- ddx-cfedee8e (escalation: wire ladder): the ladder becomes server-side orchestration logic, not loop-body logic. ADR must specify where ladder lives in the new shape.\n\nCONFLICTS WITH IN-FLIGHT REFACTOR (refactor epic ddx-5cb6e6cd children) — ADR must resolve sequencing:\n- ddx-c8f79963 (C5: no_changes adjudication move into try.Attempt): refactors the SAME code that the API transition refactors. Either land C5 first then transition, OR fold C5 into the transition.\n- ddx-06eb05d8 (C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps): same surface; same choice.\n- ddx-848069a3 (C8: routing preflight to Drain startup): related to \"server-side preflight\" decision in API design.\n- ddx-9228a484 (C9: StopCondition + cost-cap, absorbs ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-dc157075): heavy overlap. ADR must decide whether StopCondition becomes a server-side concept, a worker-side concept, or both.\n- ddx-387a0178 (C13: file/identifier rename + ddx try CLI command): the ADR's new package layout (cli/internal/agent/work/) may conflict with C13's rename plan. Coordinate.\n- ddx-1d867ec1 (rename execute_bead_loop.go): same as C13.\n\nPARALLEL-SAFE (no conflict; ADR notes them but doesn't reshape):\n- ddx-5b3e57f4 (cooldown: distinguish disrupted from gave-up): the API contract makes disruption naturally observable (server sees worker disconnect → marks Disrupted). But the cooldown logic itself is independent. ADR should call out that the API design enables cleaner Disrupted classification.\n- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute upstream gap): independent of API design.\n- ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1, ddx-055e8d32 LAYER 2, ddx-5ae050dc LAYER 3 (GraphQL project scoping): all in cli/internal/server/graphql/. Independent of worker API.\n- ddx-9d55601f (picker bug): already fixed.\n- ddx-a946c744 + ddx-aee651ec + ddx-3e60fd84 + ddx-83440482 (REACH/checks): hook into pre_merge gate; needs to integrate with the new worker contract but doesn't block ADR.\n- All Story 6/14/15/16 frontend + backend feature beads: independent.\n\nSPEC UPDATES THIS ADR DRIVES (the ADR roadmap must include these as deliverables)\n\n- New ADR document: docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md\n- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — major amendment: worker API contract section, --local-collapses-to-API explanation, deprecation of direct execution paths\n- FEAT-010 (Executions) — amendment: worker lifecycle now spans across server restarts; \"execution\" becomes a bead-attempt regardless of which worker performs it\n- SD-024 (Resolved Config / CLIOverrides) — possible amendment: if ResolvedConfig becomes part of the API request payload, SD-024 reshapes\n- TD-030 (Backend interface + chaos contract) — cross-reference if any\n- CONTRACT-003 (Fizeau Service) — no change required (fizeau is below the worker; the API is between worker and ddx-server, not worker and fizeau)\n\nNOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS ADR\n- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads from the roadmap)\n- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)\n- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)\n- Reshaping the bead schema (out of scope; ADR-004 owns that)\n\nOUTPUTS (TD deliverables)\n- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md\n- Updated FEAT-006 (or proposed amendments listed)\n- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown\n- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly\n- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups\n- Overlap reconciliation table: each overlapping bead (above) classified as ABSORBED / CONFLICT-RESOLVED / PARALLEL-SAFE with explicit rationale\n\nSEQUENCING DIRECTIVE (per user direction, 2026-05-03)\n- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing\n- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads\n- C5/C7/C9 explicitly FROZEN until the ADR's roadmap establishes the new package .ddx/beads.jsonl:218:{"acceptance":"1. TestWorkInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsBead verifies cancelling the work command context during an in-flight attempt releases the claim and leaves the bead open/re-claimable.\n2. TestWorkInterrupt_DoesNotSetNoProgressCooldown verifies interrupted work does not set `execute-loop-retry-after` or increment no-progress suppression.\n3. TestWorkInterrupt_RemovesClaimLiveness verifies external claim heartbeat/liveness is removed or stale-safe after cleanup.\n4. TestTryInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsTarget verifies `ddx try \u003cid\u003e` cancellation leaves the target bead re-claimable and exits non-zero with an interrupted/signal-class outcome.\n5. TestTryInterrupt_DoesNotReportOperatorCancel verifies plain context cancellation is not classified as `operator_cancel` unless the bead cancel marker was present.\n6. TestInterruptedAfterTerminalMutation_DoesNotUndoClose verifies cancellation after a successful terminal mutation does not reopen or unclaim a closed bead.\n7. `rg -n \"context.Canceled|context_canceled|signal|operator_cancel|execute-loop-retry-after\" cli/internal/agent cli/cmd` shows cancellation paths are explicit and comments distinguish signal interruption from operator cancel.\n8. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run \"TestWorkInterrupt|TestTryInterrupt|TestInterrupted|TestCancel|TestClaim\" -count=1` passes.\n9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"278562820307c6dc13cbe116ebcd9bc931cd56b3","created_at":"2026-05-06T15:17:45.453929652Z","description":"PROBLEM\nAfter the root context is cancelled by Ctrl-C/SIGTERM, `ddx work` and `ddx try` must finish the TD-031 interruption path: preserve available evidence, classify the attempt as mechanically disrupted, release any non-terminal bead claim, remove runtime liveness state, and leave the unfinished bead re-claimable. The current code has partial context-cancellation classification but needs explicit end-to-end coverage for the CLI work/try paths.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:859-878 marks non-success reports as disrupted when ctx is cancelled, but the surrounding cleanup/unclaim behavior is only incidentally covered by generic failure branches.\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:812-846 starts and stops claim heartbeat around an attempt; interrupted attempts must stop liveness and release or clear claims deterministically.\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:797-807 creates a dispatch context and only the bead-extra cancel path forces `operator_cancel`; plain context cancellation should remain `signal`/mechanical disruption, not `operator_cancel`.\n- cli/cmd/try.go:333-342 and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1921-1940 propagate `cmd.Context()` into worker.Run but need regression tests that cancellation leaves the target bead re-claimable and reports the signal path.\n- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:289-314 defines the claim release and no-cooldown requirements for worker shutdown/interruption.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Make interrupted attempts produce a stable disrupted/interrupted report reason (for example `context_canceled` / `signal`) without counting as model no-progress.\n- Ensure the loop unclaims interrupted non-terminal beads and removes any external claim heartbeat/liveness record.\n- Ensure interrupted attempts do not set `execute-loop-retry-after` unless a separate retryable condition was already recorded before cancellation.\n- Ensure `ddx try \u003cid\u003e` reports a clear interrupted outcome and exits non-zero after cleanup.\n- Add regression tests around worker.Run plus command-level try/work cancellation paths using controllable fake executors/runners.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not preserve worktrees as resumable by default. Cleanup/retry-from-scratch is the reliable default.\n- Do not alter operator cancel-marker semantics (`cancel-requested` / `operator_cancel`).\n- Do not change successful landing/review behavior when cancellation arrives after a terminal mutation.","events_attachment":"ddx-259bb4e2/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-259bb4e2","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:beads","area:cli","kind:bug","reliability","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TD-031","interrupt"],"parent":"ddx-99a3be32","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-c5b765cd","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"agent: cleanly unwind interrupted work and try attempts","updated_at":"2026-05-06T17:26:37.476429727Z"}

Audit: any field present in cobra extraction (agent_cmd.go:1582-1605) or workers.go:25-49 NOT in this list is a bug.\n\n2. parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) exists in the new package. Called only from cobra RunE in agent_cmd.go. No parallel cobra-extraction block remains. DispatchOptions holds control-plane flags (--local, --json) and is NOT persisted to spec.json.\n\n3. Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() methods exist. Lifecycle enforced: (a) client calls Validate() before HTTP submit or inline dispatch; (b) server handleStartExecuteLoopWorker calls ApplyDefaults() then Validate() after decode, before StartExecuteLoop; (c) spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (ApplyDefaults already ran at write time).\n\n4. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2390) decodes directly into ExecuteLoopSpec; the anonymous request struct (currently lines 2395-2409) is deleted. OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev all survive the REST round-trip.\n\n5. graphql_adapters.go anonymous request struct (lines 63-76) and its parallel StartExecuteLoop literal (lines 127-141) are deleted. GraphQL dispatch goes through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as the REST handler.\n\n6. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec in cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is replaced by or aliased to ExecuteLoopSpec. spec.json files written by StartExecuteLoop use the unified struct.\n\n7. runWorker (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658) consumes spec.MaxCostUSD as the cost-cap ceiling (with escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD fallback when zero). No hardcoded DefaultMaxCostUSD reference outside the fallback case.\n\n8. OpaquePassthrough: server-submitted spec with OpaquePassthrough=true skips BOTH validation gates — workers.go:357-361 (StartExecuteLoop pre-flight) AND the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch inside runWorker. Test: TestStartExecuteLoop_OpaquePassthrough_SkipsValidation asserts that StartExecuteLoop with OpaquePassthrough=true does not call ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService and returns a WorkerRecord without error even when the harness name is unresolvable.\n\n9. Reflection round-trip test: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection in cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go (or executeloop sub-package). Uses reflect to iterate exported fields of ExecuteLoopSpec; for each field sets a non-zero sentinel value; marshals to JSON; unmarshals; asserts equality. Test fails if any exported field has a zero-value sentinel after unmarshal (catches omitempty bugs that silently drop non-default-false fields). A future added field automatically falls under coverage.\n\n10. Backward compatibility: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ load without error. Specifically: files containing legacy fields (e.g., min_tier, max_tier from older specs) are tolerated and ignored by json.Unmarshal default behavior. Do NOT enable json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads.\n\n11. Forward compatibility: SpecVersion int field present. Server returns HTTP 400 with a capability description when incoming SpecVersion \u003e server-supported version. SpecVersion==0 (unset) is treated as the current version. Test: TestHandleStartExecuteLoopWorker_HighSpecVersion_Returns400.\n\n12. Regression: --local path (runAgentExecuteLoopImpl inline path) is unchanged in behavior. When a flag is added to cobra, the developer must also update the REST handler request struct in server.go and the GraphQL adapter request struct in graphql_adapters.go; missing one silently drops the field at runtime. This is the root cause of the five flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) being dropped on the server-managed-worker path, causing \"ddx work --harness claude\" to fail harness validation when routed through the server.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nEach execute-loop flag traverses six independent declaration points with no compile-time linkage:\n\n1. cobra flag extract + local var: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 (fromRev, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once, pollInterval, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel, maxCostUSD, requestTimeout, minPower, maxPower extracted as naked locals)\n\n2. positional-param pass-down: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1744-1773 — these locals are closed over by singleTierAttempt and assembled into config.CLIOverrides by hand; no single struct groups them\n\n3. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec definition (worker-side canonical): cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 — typed, but hand-maintained parallel to cobra; missing MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev\n\n4. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker request struct (REST handler): cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 — SEPARATE anonymous struct; the five dropped flags and OpaquePassthrough are absent here, causing them to be silently lost for server-spawned workers\n\n5. StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 + cli/internal/server/workers.go:345-361 — hand-assembled from the request struct fields; any field absent in step 4 cannot appear here\n\n6. GraphQL adapter request struct + StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 + 127-141 — a THIRD parallel declaration (OpaquePassthrough absent here too); fixes to the REST path leave GraphQL re-broken\n\nThe consumer (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-926 runWorker) reads MaxCostUSD from escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD hardcoded (line ~753) instead of spec.MaxCostUSD because the field was never plumbed through. OpaquePassthrough has TWO validation skip gates that must both fire (workers.go:357-361 and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch in runWorker).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- cli/internal/agent/spec.go (new file): define ExecuteLoopSpec with ALL fields currently spread across the six layers. Fields: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Once, PollInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion int. JSON tags drive both REST wire format and disk persistence.\n- cli/internal/agent/spec.go: add Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() methods. Add parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) — the single cobra→spec conversion.\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605: replace naked-local extraction with parseExecuteLoopSpec call. Remove positional-param closure pattern; pass spec by value.\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49: replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with (or alias to) ExecuteLoopSpec from the new package. Delete the parallel struct.\n- cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409: delete the anonymous request struct in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker; decode directly into ExecuteLoopSpec. Call ApplyDefaults() then Validate() before StartExecuteLoop.\n- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 + 127-141: delete the parallel anonymous struct and parallel StartExecuteLoop literal; route through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as the REST handler.\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go:~753: replace hardcoded escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD with spec.MaxCostUSD (with DefaultMaxCostUSD as fallback when zero).\n- cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go (new file): TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection uses reflect over exported fields; sets non-zero value per field; marshals; unmarshals; asserts round-trip. New field = automatic coverage.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7) owns replacing runWorker with exec ddx work; this bead does NOT change the subprocess vs in-process dispatch decision.\n- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, MCP agent dispatch) — same smell may exist; covered by sister bead ddx-fb290074.\n- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.\n- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — owned by ddx-9d55601f; keep LabelFilter as string.\n\nSEQUENCING\nddx-29058e2a (this bead) vs ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7 — server-spawn migration to exec ddx work):\nLANDS INDEPENDENTLY and BEFORE ddx-eccc6efb. ADR-022 §\"Sequencing relative to in-flight work\" (line 553) is explicit: \"[ddx-29058e2a] remains valuable as a worker-side struct unification (cobra → in-process flow) and should land independently.\" When ddx-eccc6efb lands later, it will delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec from workers.go as part of the exec migration — but by then, the unified ExecuteLoopSpec already lives in cli/internal/agent/spec.go, which is the correct durable home for it regardless of transport. ddx-eccc6efb does NOT block or supersede this bead; this bead does NOT block ddx-eccc6efb. agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=8503 cost_usd=0.8610"},{"actor":"erik","body":"Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits. ## Summary The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector. These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either: (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned. The bead's \"SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS\" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make. ## Evidence 1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims). 2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: \"Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands.\" Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`. 3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites). 4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (\"ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server\") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec. 5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands. ## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict) The bead also fails the size check on its own: - 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run). - Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals. - File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. .ddx/beads.jsonl:254:{"acceptance":"1. TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedStopsLoop creates two ready beads and an executor returning resource_exhausted for the first; verifies the second bead is never claimed or executed.\n2. TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedUnclaimsAndNoCooldown verifies any claimed bead is open/re-claimable and no execute-loop-retry-after/no-progress cooldown is written.\n3. TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedLoopEndEvent verifies loop.end includes exit_reason=resource_exhausted and cleanup/resource detail is emitted.\n4. TestWorkResourceExhausted_PrintsOperatorMessage verifies the ddx work command output includes resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop or the finalized exact message.\n5. Resource exhaustion remains distinct from review transport/quota and provider rate-limit retry in status/detail handling.\n6. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run \"TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhausted|TestWorkResourceExhausted\" -count=1 passes.\n7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"aef8564903a026b3e8a0dcedfdbc96dced157261","created_at":"2026-05-06T15:36:40.874557442Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-2d49729e","depends_on_id":"ddx-8fa7ecf6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-06T15:37:49Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nddx work currently continues scanning and claiming ready beads after setup/infrastructure failures. During the May 6 ENOSPC incident, ddx work failed to create one isolated worktree, then immediately claimed another production-reachability bead and failed again. Resource exhaustion must stop the drain because continuing expands the blast radius.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 initializes loop events and exitReason but does not include resource_exhausted as a recognized loop-fatal exit reason.\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:529 onward continues iterating after non-success reports unless existing retry/no-progress/once logic stops it; there is no host-resource circuit breaker.\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:263-283 defines status taxonomy around execution_failed/no_evidence/etc., but there is no distinct resource_exhausted status/reason for loop policy to inspect.\n- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:434-440 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:281-284 require ddx work to stop claiming new beads after resource_exhausted.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add a stable resource_exhausted attempt/loop classification that can be returned by ddx try/resource preflight and recognized by ExecuteBeadWorker.\n- On resource_exhausted, release any non-terminal claim, emit cleanup/resource events, set loop.end exit_reason=resource_exhausted, and return without scanning the next ready bead.\n- Print an operator-visible message such as resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop.\n- Ensure resource_exhausted is not counted as model no-progress, capability retry, or review/quota retry.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not implement the resource checker itself here; this bead consumes the outcome from the try/resource-preflight bead.\n- Do not change queue ranking or production-reachability bead priority.\n- Do not change provider 429 in-attempt retry behavior.","events_attachment":"ddx-2d49729e/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-2d49729e","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:cli","kind:bug","reliability","spec:FEAT-010","spec:SD-025","resource-exhaustion","cleanup"],"parent":"ddx-f3dfde0d","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-5799042e","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"work: stop queue drain on resource_exhausted attempts","updated_at":"2026-05-06T17:44:20.412845599Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:308:{"acceptance":"1. **Root cause #1 fixed:** `attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}` is moved to AFTER the pre-claim hook succeeds. Regression test in `execute_bead_loop_test.go` asserts: with a fake `PreClaimHook` that fails on the first call and succeeds thereafter, the loop processes the same bead on the second iteration (not skipped because of stale `attempted` state).\n\n2. **Root cause #2 fixed:** the post-`Execute` outcome-handling block (lines ~510-862) does not exit the loop on transient `Store.*` errors. Each `return result, err` in that block is converted to: log the error, emit a `kind:loop-error` bead event when possible, set the bead to a short cooldown, and `continue` to the next iteration. The only exception is context cancellation (`ctx.Err()`), which still terminates immediately.\n\n3. **Regression tests for AC-2:** for each `Store.*` call in the outcome block (Unclaim, IncrNoChangesCount, adjudicateNoChanges, CloseWithEvidence on each path, Reopen on each path, SetExecutionCooldown on each path, late AppendEvent), a test injects a transient error from a fake store and asserts: (a) the loop continues, (b) a `kind:loop-error` event is recorded with the failing operation name, (c) `result.Failures++` advances.\n\n4. **End-to-end reproduction guard:** an integration test seeds a bead.jsonl with 3 ready beads. The first returns `no_changes` (executor fake), the second returns `success`, the third returns `already_satisfied`. The loop runs to completion (all three processed) without exiting prematurely. With the pre-fix loop, this test fails on the first bead.\n\n5. **Worker-state semantics preserved:** existing tests that assert worker.State transitions (`running` → `exited` / `failed` / `stopped` / `reaped`) still pass. The fix changes loop *advancement*, not terminal-state classification.\n\n6. **Documented behavior:** `docs/agents/execute-loop.md` (or the closest existing doc) gains a \"loop termination\" section listing the only conditions that terminate the loop: context cancellation, `Once == true`, `PollInterval \u003c= 0` AND `nextCandidate` returns no candidate, `RoutePreflight` rejected. Explicitly states that Store errors during outcome handling do NOT terminate the loop.\n\n7. **Operator workaround retired:** the README's \"if `ddx work` exits early, restart it\" guidance (or equivalent) is removed; that workaround was masking this bug.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"1495ba706ffdc0e7bfb4833487ed068ffe732aeb","created_at":"2026-04-29T19:05:38.552407002Z","description":"## Symptom\n\n`ddx work` (no `--once` flag, no `--poll-interval`, default mode) exits after processing exactly one bead instead of draining the queue. Reproduced four times today on `/home/erik/Projects/agent` with `--harness lmstudio` pinned (which sidesteps the unrelated `ddx-c7081f89` server-worker tier-walk issue):\n\n| Run | Outcome on first bead | Worker terminal state | Queue still non-empty? |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| 2 | `already_satisfied` (`agent-4aa3584e` adjudicated satisfied) | `already_satisfied` / `exited` | yes — 2 ready beads remained |\n| 3 | pre-claim hook rejected (diverged branch) | `exited`, attempts=0 | yes |\n| 4 | pre-claim hook rejected (diverged branch) | `exited`, attempts=0 | yes |\n| 5 | `no_changes` from agent (`agent-6c7a4c11` declined as epic; bead left **claimed**) | `exited`, attempts=1 | yes — 5 ready beads remained |\n\nIn all four runs the loop should have continued to the next ready bead. Per `agent/.claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md`, only `success`/`already_satisfied` close the bead and `no_changes`/`land_conflict`/`*_failed` unclaim and continue. None of these outcomes is documented or expected to be loop-terminal.\n\n## Root cause #1 (verified)\n\n`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:382`:\n\n```go\nattempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}\n\n// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.\n// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop\n// continues (ctx is not cancelled).\nif runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {\n    if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {\n        // ... log + emit ...\n        continue\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThe bead is added to `attempted` **before** the pre-claim hook runs. On hook failure (e.g. diverged branch), the loop `continue`s but the bead stays in `attempted`. Next iteration, `nextCandidate()` (line 924) skips the still-ready bead because it's in `attempted`, returns `ok=false`, and the loop exits at line 374 because `PollInterval \u003c= 0`. Single-shot exit with `attempts=0`.\n\n**Reproduces:** runs 3 and 4 above.\n\n## Root cause #2 (suspected — needs targeted instrumentation)\n\nRuns 2 and 5 reached `Execute()` and got a real report back. Worker log shows the entry line `▶ \u003cbead\u003e: \u003ctitle\u003e` but no closing `✓ \u003cbead\u003e → \u003cresult\u003e` line (which is written at line 909). That means the loop exited somewhere between line 488 (`Executor.Execute`) and line 909 (the final summary log).\n\nBetween those bounds, **at least 16 distinct `return result, err` points** trigger if any `Store.*` operation returns an error: `Unclaim` (711, 700), `IncrNoChangesCount` (717), `adjudicateNoChanges` (721), `CloseWithEvidence` (520, 615, 694, 739), `Reopen` (635, 669), `SetExecutionCooldown` (749, 791, 803, 835, 844), late `AppendEvent` (862). Any single transient store error during outcome handling kills the worker.\n\nFor run 5 specifically, `agent-6c7a4c11` is now `Status: in_progress` with `Owner: ddx, Machine: sindri`. The bead was **never unclaimed** despite the agent returning `no_changes` — meaning the loop exited on or before line 711 (`w.Store.Unclaim`) failing. Investigation should look at what error `Unclaim` would return when called against a bead the same worker claimed seconds earlier; possible candidates: tracker-write race with concurrent worker, bead.jsonl re-write during a fetch by the pre-claim hook on a later iteration that already happened (no — pre-claim is pre-claim, not post-execute), or a malformed bead row that fails store-side validation.\n\nIndependent of the specific store error: the structural pattern of \"any Store.* error → exit the entire loop\" is the wrong default. A store error on outcome handling should at most park the bead and continue to the next, never terminate the worker.\n\n## Why this is loadbearing\n\nThe loop is the queue-drain primitive. Every observed failure-to-drain in the last 24 hours of operator complaints traces back to this — beads that should have been the worker's second/third/fourth attempt never got picked up because the worker exited after one attempt, and the operator manually restarts the drain (sometimes after a long delay). The visible queue depth is misleading: half the \"ready\" beads are sitting behind a worker that quietly died.\n\n## In-scope files\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` — fixes for both root causes\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go` — regression tests for each path\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Anything in the `agent` SDK (`/home/erik/Projects/agent`). The loop and all its store interactions are owned by ddx.\n- The pre-claim hook's divergence-detection logic itself — that's correct behavior; the bug is how the loop reacts to the hook's signal.\n- Adaptive min-tier / tier-walk machinery (`ddx-c7081f89`, `ddx-aec9d68c`) — independent.\n\n## Anti-goals\n\n- Do **not** swallow store errors silently. They must be surfaced (event + log) but should not kill the worker on transient/per-bead failures.\n- Do **not** convert the loop's Once/PollInterval semantics — they're correct.\n- Do **not** change worker terminal-state semantics (`exited`/`failed`/`stopped`/`reaped`).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"resolved_model\":\"sonnet\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:58:01.9171561Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"attempt_id\":\"20260429T203909-e46a1ac0\",\"harness\":\"claude\",\"model\":\"sonnet\",\"input_tokens\":92,\"output_tokens\":47611,\"total_tokens\":47703,\"cost_usd\":3.409703350000001,\"duration_ms\":1131913,\"exit_code\":0}","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:58:02.023531375Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=47703 cost_usd=3.4097 model=sonnet"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"escalation_count\":0,\"fallback_chain\":[],\"final_tier\":\"\",\"requested_profile\":\"\",\"requested_tier\":\"\",\"resolved_model\":\"sonnet\",\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"resolved_tier\":\"\"}","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:58:06.183307339Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude model=sonnet"},{"actor":"erik","body":"Diff only contains execution evidence (manifest.json + result.json) under .ddx/executions/. .ddx/beads.jsonl:325:{"acceptance":"1. Loop has triage step after each attempt's terminal event. 2. Per-failure-mode triage policy in .ddx/config.yaml. 3. Triage outcomes (re-attempt-with-context, escalate-tier, needs_human) align exactly with TD-031 TriageContract (per ddx-673833f4). **No new persisted statuses introduced — all triage state lives in labels (e.g., triage:needs-human) and events per bd/br compatibility (ADR-004).** 4. Default: BLOCK→re-attempt-with-context, then escalate-tier, then add 'triage:needs-human' label. 5. Lock-error handling delegates to ddx-da11a34a's implementation; this bead does NOT duplicate retry/backoff logic. Triage policy entry for lock errors invokes the lock-bead's published API. 6. Tests: BLOCK→re-attempt, BLOCK→escalate, lock-error→delegates-to-lock-bead, no_changes→re-evaluate. 7. Out of scope: rate-limit handling, quota-pause handling, lock-retry implementation, status-enum modification. 8. needs_human stays a LABEL per current code (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go:19-23) and TD's resolution. 9. **schema_compat_test.go round-trip passes after this bead lands.**","closing_commit_sha":"4221a8f3596a23aedd4bab355865d023c9b3f344","created_at":"2026-05-02T13:04:11.868818227Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-3c154349","depends_on_id":"ddx-673833f4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T18:14:01Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-3c154349","depends_on_id":"ddx-7d7a17c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T19:04:17Z"}],"description":"Currently when a bead hits a post-merge review BLOCK, the drain loop emits the BLOCK event, leaves the bead open, and moves to the next ready bead. The BLOCK then sits in the queue waiting for a human. Same for many substantive execution_failed cases — no automated triage.\n\nThis bead handles BLOCK + substantive failures only. Two SEPARATE beads cover related-but-distinct concerns:\n- ddx-aede917d: drain pause/resume on quota exhaustion (Fizeau-side state, DDx consumes typed error)\n- ddx-c6e3db02: per-bead rate-limit retry honoring Retry-After (provider stays in rotation)\n\nFailure modes triaged HERE:\n- post-merge review BLOCK → either (a) re-attempt-with-context (BLOCK reasons embedded in next prompt), (b) escalate to next tier in the S10_2 ladder, or (c) file follow-up bead capturing BLOCK reasons + mark original needs_human\n- 'staging tracker: Unable to create .git/index.lock' (git contention — see  / ddx-da11a34a) → retry with backoff after lock clears\n- substantive execution_failed (probe_ok+exec_fail; agent finished but produced no useful diff or wrong shape) → escalate per S10 ladder\n- 'no_changes' (agent decided nothing to do) → re-evaluate against the bead AC; if still seems valid, file a clarification follow-up rather than mark already_satisfied\n\nTriage policy is config-driven, per-failure-mode mapping in .ddx/config.yaml triage:. Default: BLOCK→re-attempt-with-context, then escalate-tier on second BLOCK, then needs_human on third.\n\nNOT in scope: rate-limit handling (ddx-c6e3db02) and quota-pause handling (ddx-aede917d) — those are separate beads with their own infrastructure.","events":[{"actor":"","body":"","created_at":"2026-05-02T18:09:03.619482987Z","kind":"reopen","source":"","summary":"Auto-closed by drain with only execution-evidence files (no implementation). FEAT-001 removes the `ddx agent` structural mount and defines only `ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx work`, and any Fizeau diagnostics namespace that remains, with no aliases.\n2. FEAT-006 is renamed or rewritten as the DDx consumer spec for Fizeau, and no longer presents `agentlib.DdxAgent` / `ddx-agent` as the desired contract name.\n3. FEAT-010 names DDx-owned packages/roles using task execution vocabulary (`run`, `try`, `work`, `taskexec`, `fizeauadapter`) rather than `internal/agent`.\n4. ADR-024 says escalation changes only MinPower/MaxPower/request facts and Fizeau chooses the concrete route.\n5. TP-020 is replaced or superseded with a DDx boundary test plan proving DDx does not normalize, fuzzy-match, or route models.\n6. docs/migrations/routing-config.md documents removal of `agent.*` defaults and the split between Fizeau routing config and DDx execution config.\n7. DDx skill references explain explicit passthrough and Fizeau-owned model matching using examples such as `--model qwen36`, while stating DDx passes the raw string unchanged.\n8. `rg -n \"ddx agent|ddx-agent|agentlib.DdxAgent|Agent Service|cli/internal/agent\" docs .agents/skills .claude/skills skills` returns only audited historical or literal-external-agent hits documented in the change.\n9. `ddx doc validate` passes or any unrelated pre-existing failures are listed in the bead notes.\n10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-05-06T16:44:49.87755234Z","description":"PROBLEM\nImplementation agents need durable specs before removing `ddx agent`, renaming DDx internals, or consuming Fizeau contract changes. FEAT-006 still describes the upstream invocation boundary as `agentlib.DdxAgent` and `DDx Agent Service` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:1-23. FEAT-010 still says layer 1 consumes upstream `ddx-agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:16-19. TP-020 remains a historical DDx-side routing test plan even though Fizeau owns routing. docs/migrations/routing-config.md:1-13 removes some old routing keys but does not yet document the full `agent.*` removal / Fizeau-vs-execution config split.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nUpdate the specs before implementation. The specs must state: DDx owns task execution (`run`, `try`, `work`); Fizeau owns routing, provider/model discovery, native transcript/session rendering, and fuzzy model constraint resolution; `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are explicit operator passthrough only; DDx passes raw strings unchanged; `ddx agent` is removed without deprecation; and `agent` is allowed only for actual external AI agent concepts.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nNo production code removal in this bead. No Fizeau implementation work. No compatibility alias design. .ddx/beads.jsonl:356:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go implements NewPostAttemptTriageHook or an equivalently named constructor returning func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error), with explicit injected AgentRunner/service/config inputs and no dependency on cli/cmd.\n2. TestTriageHook_UsesRunnerLibrary asserts the hook reaches an injected AgentRunner.Run path and a static grep/AST assertion proves triage_hook.go does not import os/exec or shell out to ddx agent run.\n3. TestTriageHook_PromptIncludesOutcomeAndLogExcerpt verifies the prompt starts with MODE: triage, names bead-lifecycle, includes bead content, ExecuteBeadReport JSON, and a bounded session-log excerpt when available.\n4. TestLoop_TriageHook_FiresPostOutcome proves PostAttemptTriageHook runs after report status/detail are populated and before no-progress cooldown is applied.\n5. TestTriageHook_RecordsButDoesNotMutateOutcome proves valid triage classification populates OutcomeReason and/or a triage event without changing ExecuteBeadReport.Status.\n6. TestTriageHook_HookError_FallsThroughToLegacyCooldown proves hook error leaves OutcomeReason empty and preserves existing cooldown behavior.\n7. The triage prompt requests structured JSON fields classification, recommended_action, rationale, suggested_amendments, and suggested_followup_beads.\n8. WIRED-IN: git grep PostAttemptTriageHook cli/internal/agent shows the execute-loop invocation site, not just the runtime field declaration.\n9. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... green.\n10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"9d6892f1b33e72b695119fddcbb54575e0d984c3","created_at":"2026-05-04T15:37:15.580462478Z","description":"PROBLEM\n  ddx try needs post-attempt triage after the loop has adjudicated an attempt outcome and before cooldown decisions, so transient failures can be classified without mutating the underlying status. Today the loop records status/detail/rationale but has no triage hook invocation path that can populate OutcomeReason.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:940-1090 contains the outcome adjudication and cooldown branch chain. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:976 and 1084 call shouldSuppressNoProgress before applying no-progress cooldown. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1099 appends the report to the loop result after cooldown handling. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:30 now exposes ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PostAttemptTriageHook and cli/internal/agent/quality_hooks.go defines TriageResult, but no cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go exists and the loop never calls the hook.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n  - Add cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go with a constructor, e.g. NewPostAttemptTriageHook(projectRoot string, store BeadReader, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, svc agentlib.FizeauService, runner AgentRunner, sessionLogReader SessionLogExcerptReader) func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error). If package boundaries require a different name, preserve the explicit injected inputs.\n  - The hook builds a prompt whose first line is MODE: triage, names bead-lifecycle, includes the bead body, ExecuteBeadReport as JSON, the most relevant recent bead events, and a bounded session-log excerpt when report.SessionID is non-empty and a log can be found under the project agent log dir.\n  - Invoke dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx, projectRoot, svc, runner, rcfg, AgentRunRuntime{Prompt: prompt, WorkDir: projectRoot, PromptSource: \"bead-lifecycle-triage\"}) or an equivalent package-local wrapper that still uses AgentRunner/ResolvedConfig and not os/exec.\n  - In execute_bead_loop.go, call runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook after report.Status/Detail/Rationale/BaseRev/ResultRev are final for the attempt and before any shouldSuppressNoProgress or SetExecutionCooldown branch. A valid classification sets report.OutcomeReason = TriageResult.Classification and records triage details in the execute-bead event body or a dedicated bead-quality.triage event.\n  - On hook failure, fail open: leave OutcomeReason empty and keep legacy cooldown behavior.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n  - Defining OutcomeReason and TriageResult; closed in ddx-c8dc1146.\n  - CLI wiring for ddx try; covered by sibling/parent wiring work.\n  - GraphQL exposure of OutcomeReason.\n  - Automatically creating SuggestedFollowupBeads; record/surface only unless a later bead opts in.\n\nPARENT\n  ddx-e1a576a7.\n\nDEPS\n  No deps. ddx-c8dc1146 is closed and the OutcomeReason/TriageResult contract .ddx/beads.jsonl:360:{"acceptance":"- [ ] ddx bead reopen command exists\n- [ ] Status flip + notes append + event recorded atomically\n- [ ] Unit + integration tests\n- [ ] Used by the post-review path from gap #1","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T04:57:51Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"8e0b77a980455d4cea392d42ab4e49b01541bc95","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:48.573308839Z","description":"Reopening a closed bead after a failing review currently takes 3-4 separate commands: update --status open, update --notes (append), optionally update --description, optionally update --acceptance. This is error-prone and loses the atomicity guarantee that a reopen is a single logical event.\n\n## Scope\n- ddx bead reopen \u003cid\u003e [--reason \u003ctext\u003e] [--append-notes \u003ctext\u003e]\n- Atomically: set status=open, append a reopen event to the event log, optionally append notes, clear claim fields\n- Emit an event of kind=reopen that shows in ddx bead show\n- Works from CLI and from ddx agent execute-loop review-rejection path\n\n## Context\nObserved during axon ADR-018 loop: first attempt merged, opus review rejected, I had to run three ddx bead update commands plus a description rewrite to get the bead back into a ready-for-second-attempt state. An atomic reopen would have been one command.","events":[{"actor":"audit-reviewer","body":"This bead is one of the review-loop primitives the meta-epic depends on.
