ExecuteBeadReport gains an explicit Disrupted bool (or Disposition variant) set when the loop detects: ctx.Err() != nil, executor exited via SIGKILL/SIGTERM, transport-class provider error, or worker process restart marker.\n2. Detection at: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go where ExecutionFailed is set (current lines 513 routing-preflight, 629/637 executor-error, 1011 in execute_bead.go) — each set point evaluates whether the failure is Disrupted.\n3. The cooldown branch (lines 1074-1086) checks Disrupted FIRST: if Disrupted, skip SetExecutionCooldown entirely; just Unclaim so the bead returns to ready.\n4. shouldSuppressNoProgress unchanged for genuine no-progress (BaseRev == ResultRev AND not Disrupted AND no rationale).\n5. New diagnostic event `disruption_detected` with kind, reason, and bead context — emitted when Disrupted is set so operators can see disruption rates.\n6. (Optional) Exponential backoff for non-Disrupted no-progress: first failure 5min; second 30min; third 2h; cap at 24h. Reset on success. If implementing: replace flat noProgressCooldown with this curve; document in code comment.\n7. Tests:\n   - TestLoop_DisruptedExecution_NoCooldown — context cancelled mid-execution; assert no execute-loop-retry-after set; bead returns to ready\n   - TestLoop_GenuineNoProgress_StillCooldowns — model returns clean with no commit + rationale; assert 6h cooldown set\n   - TestLoop_PreflightRejection_NoCooldown — routing preflight fails; assert disruption classification (operator config, not model failure)\n   - TestLoop_DisruptionEventEmitted — assert disruption_detected event appears in bead events\n8. Manual verification: kill an in-flight worker mid-execution; assert the bead is immediately re-claimable by the next worker (no 6h park).\n9. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.\n10. Documentation: update FEAT-006 (or wherever the cooldown policy is documented) to describe Disrupted vs no-progress and the policy rationale.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:17:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"163374","closing_commit_sha":"89473d084d436e925934549d31f560e5d6b8702a","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:47:21.609526243Z","description":"The current cooldown logic treats any ExecutionFailed status as evidence the model can't make progress, applying noProgressCooldown=6h when BaseRev == ResultRev (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1074-1086 via shouldSuppressNoProgress at line 1550). This silently freezes important work whenever a worker is disrupted (killed, server restart, network blip, OS issue) — disruption produces ExecutionFailed + no commit, indistinguishable from \"model genuinely tried and produced nothing.\"\n\nOBSERVED (today, 2026-05-02)\nAfter the picker fix investigation found the four \"starved\" P0 beads (ddx-dc157075, ddx-aee651ec, ddx-29058e2a, ddx-9d55601f) all had execute-loop-retry-after timestamps ~3 hours in the future. Investigation per ddx-9d55601f confirmed these were NOT genuine model failures — they were prior worker disruptions during this development session (worker kills, server restarts) being treated as model-gave-up.\n\nUser directive: \"Cooldown should be reserved for cases where the model is truly unable to make progress on a task.\"\n\nCURRENT COOLDOWN POLICY (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)\n- LandConflictCooldown = 15min — short cooldown after merge/land conflicts (line 64)\n- noProgressCooldown = 6h default — when shouldSuppressNoProgress is true (line 367)\n- MaxLoopCooldown = 24h — push_failed, push_conflict, decomposition decline (line 57)\n\nshouldSuppressNoProgress (line 1550): returns true if BaseRev != \"\" AND ResultRev != \"\" AND BaseRev == ResultRev. This catches:\n- Model wrote nothing AND came back cleanly (true no_progress) ✓\n- Worker killed mid-execution before commit (FALSE no_progress — disruption)\n- Network/provider error before model started (FALSE no_progress — disruption)\n- Routing preflight rejection (FALSE no_progress — config issue)\n\nThe mis-classification is the bug.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n\nAdd a Disposition::Disrupted variant (or equivalent flag on ExecuteBeadReport) that explicitly marks results that are NOT evidence of model inability:\n- ctx.Err() != nil (cancellation, deadline, parent died)\n- syscall.SIGTERM / SIGINT received during execution\n- Provider-side 5xx, connection refused, timeout (transport disruption)\n- Worker process killed (parent never received clean result)\n- Server-restart-detected marker\n- Routing preflight rejection (operator config issue, not model failure)\n\nFor Disrupted: skip cooldown entirely (or use a tiny defer like 30s) and unclaim the bead so the next worker pickup is immediate.\nFor genuine no-progress (model returned clean with no commit AND rationale present): keep current 6h cooldown.\n\nALTERNATIVE: exponential backoff per bead. First failure: 5min; second: 30min; third: 2h; cap at 24h. Resets on any successful attempt or operator clear. This is more forgiving than the current flat 6h on the first hiccup.\n\nPROBABLY BOTH: Disrupted skips cooldown entirely; non-Disrupted failures use exponential backoff starting at 5min, not flat 6h.\n\nDETECTION POINTERS\n\n- ctx.Err() check at the call site that produces ExecutionFailed (line 513, 629, 637, 1011)\n- Check for `.ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/no_changes_rationale.txt` presence — if missing, the model never wrote one (likely disruption)\n- Check the executor's exit code: SIGKILL/SIGTERM = disruption; clean exit with empty result = no-progress\n- Track parent process restart count or boot ID; if changed since the attempt started, mark Disrupted\n\nNOT IN SCOPE\n- Changing the cooldown duration constants themselves\n- Reworking the per-bead retry policy framework (separate concern)\n- Changing land-conflict or push-failed cooldowns (those are correct as-is)\n- The exponential-backoff alternative is OPTIONAL; primary fix is Disrupted classification\n\nINTERSECTION WITH OTHER BEADS\n- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): the stay-alive fix prevents some classes of disruption (worker no longer exits on empty poll), but does NOT fix the cooldown-on-disruption mis-classification\n- ddx-076147ee (worker client-server architecture TD): if workers become long-lived clients, server-restart disruption goes away by construction. But this fix is needed BEFORE that TD is implemented because the disruption classification is needed regardless.\n- ddx-9228a484 C9 (StopCondition enum): may absorb this — the StopCondition enum could include Disrupted as a state. The user undercounted the smell by ~5×. The implicit state machine in the loop is real and coherent (Pick → Gate → Claim → Execute → Classify → Reviewing → Terminal) but written as conditional soup instead of explicit state.\n\n## Three-package split aligned to FEAT-010\n\n  cli/internal/agent/run/   — Layer 1: invocation atom (existing dispatch + runner)\n  cli/internal/agent/try/   — Layer 2: bead attempt (worktree, prompt, single Execute, Land, conflict-recovery, post-merge review, triage)\n  cli/internal/agent/work/  — Layer 3: queue drain (pick, claim, heartbeat, drain-loop control, stop conditions)\n\ntry.Attempt(beadID) returns sealed Outcome { Disposition (enum), Cooldown, ParkReason, RecordEvents }. Loop becomes ~30 lines: pick → guard → claim → attemptWithHeartbeat → applyOutcome. Single commitOutcome helper deletes the 18 store-error returns.\n\n## Ordering: refactor-LAST per user decision\n\nIn-flight Group A beads (c6e3db02 rate-limit, b24e9630 no_changes, cfedee8e ladder wire, a8718bec cost-cap reviewer) land first against current code. dc157075 tactical worker-stay-alive lands first as operational bridge. THIS refactor lands last and absorbs all intervening behavior into the new architecture. Per-child ACs explicitly call out which in-flight bead's behavior each child must preserve.\n\n## Naming\n\nPackage = try (not try_bead — Go idiom). Function = try.Attempt(beadID). Loop = work.Worker.Drain. CLI surface (ddx run / try / work) already correct; C13 also ADDS ddx try as a new CLI command (FEAT-010 named it but it doesn't exist yet).\n\n## Children\n\n  C0  Pre-refactor JSONL fixture baseline for event-shape regression detection\n  C1  try.Outcome sealed sum type + Disposition enum + ParkReason vocabulary enumeration\n  C2  try.Attempt function shell (wraps existing executor; no behavior change)\n  C3  Move review verdict handling into try.Attempt (180 lines from loop body)\n  C4  Move conflict recovery into try.Attempt\n  C5  Move no_changes adjudication, decomposition, push-failed, push-conflict (absorbs ddx-b24e9630, ddx-c6e3db02 behavior)\n  C6  Single commitOutcome helper; deletes 18 store-error exit paths + handleOutcomeStoreError\n  C7  Uniform Guard contract; delete attempted/hookFailed maps; pick() derives from drain.result\n  C8  Routing preflight moves to Drain startup (runs once, not per iteration)\n  C9  First-class StopCondition enum + cost-cap relocation (absorbs ddx-cfedee8e ladder + ddx-a8718bec reviewer cost + ddx-dc157075 tactical work-stay-alive). Includes downstream-consumer audit for execution_failed:max-cost grep before relocation. Includes 30s default for ddx work --poll-interval.\n  C10 ddx work stay-alive default + transient idle substate (subsumes ddx-dc157075 fully)\n  C11 Heartbeat extracted to withHeartbeat helper\n  C12 Single phase-event emission (replaces 4 sites)\n  C13 File and identifier rename per §3 of proposal + ADD ddx try CLI command + handle dc157075 file-path migration if it landed first\n  C14 Delete ExecuteBead* shims; verify all callers ported off (grep audit)","id":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","issue_type":"epic","labels":["phase:2","refactor","area:agent"],"notes":"Historical refactor epic remains useful as context, but the no-agent cleanup sequence is now governed by ddx-f088a3fd. Any future implementation must follow the new package/vocabulary target in ddx-b194d20f rather than creating new cli/internal/agent packages.","parent":"ddx-b18d9ce6","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"REFACTOR: execute-bead surface → try / work packages aligned to FEAT-010","updated_at":"2026-05-06T16:46:42.095616034Z"}

Events array per bead is unbounded and dominates archive size\n3. Cross-machine concurrency is not addressed (Story 14 federation will require it)\n4. The current model bakes one process-local store + git-as-replication; doesn't compose cleanly with a real database\n\nTwo backend paths under consideration:\n\n**Axon (PRIMARY, per user inclination)**: integrate as the first DDx-side consumer of axon. User owns the stack; tight integration possible; proves axon's value as a substrate. Requires authoring an axon backend implementing the bead schema's storage contract (per ADR-004 / SD-004 — collection abstraction, claim semantics, atomic writes, JSONL interchange compatibility for bd/br).\n\n**bd / doltdb (FALLBACK)**: delegate to bd which already speaks doltdb (git-for-data). Trivial integration; downside is tracking an external codebase as a hard dependency.\n\nThis epic frames the evaluation, designs the integration, prototypes, and migrates. Children break down the work. .ddx/beads.jsonl:489:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-30T03:39:42.445334383Z","description":"Finding from fresh-eyes review of DDx-owned retry semantics.\n\nProblem:\nThe current DDx plan correctly says DDx owns success classification and retry, but it does not define a hard eligibility matrix for when a failed run may be retried with higher MinPower. The upstream agent contract distinguishes capability/semantic failures from deterministic setup failures and says dirty worktrees, merge conflicts, invalid bead metadata, unresolved dependencies, config parse errors, missing binaries, auth failures, and command-not-found cases are not eligible for power retry.\n\nWhy this matters:\nWithout an explicit matrix, DDx can burn stronger models on failures where model power is irrelevant. That keeps DDx involved in agent execution behavior in an ad hoc way and makes retry outcomes impossible to audit.\n\nRequired fix:\nExtend FEAT-010 and ddx-9f82f325 with a retry eligibility table. DDx may escalate MinPower only for classified outcomes where more capable reasoning could plausibly change the result, using agent status/output as evidence but not inspecting provider/model routing details. Deterministic environment, tracker, dependency, config, auth, missing-binary, and passthrough-unsatisfied failures must stop as blocked/operator-action-required.\n\nAcceptance:\n- Retry policy has named eligible and ineligible status classes.\n- Tests cover at least one capability failure that escalates MinPower and several deterministic failures that do not.\n- Audit records include the classification reason used for retry/stop.\n- The policy never branches on provider/model identity except for passthrough constraint exhaustion reported by the agent as a typed status.","id":"ddx-6232daf5","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:agent","area:execution"],"parent":"ddx-deb9be7e","priority":1,"spec_id":"review:power-passthrough-boundary-retry-eligibility","status":"closed","title":"review finding: define retry eligibility before escalating agent power","updated_at":"2026-04-30T03:50:03.4810409Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:500:{"acceptance":"1. TestPreCloseReview_FixableGapSchedulesRepair verifies review_fixable_gap prevents close and the next implementation cycle receives repair_context_from_review_group.\n2. TestPreCloseReview_TerminalClassificationsBlockOperator verifies spec_gap, missing_acceptance, too_large at depth cap, unsafe_or_out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error block with needs_human/review-manual-required.\n3. TestPreCloseReview_ReviewErrorRetryBudgetPerSlot verifies retry accounting is keyed by result_rev and reviewer_index.\n4. TestPreCloseReview_ReviewOutcomesDoNotConsumeNoProgress verifies review_error and operator-blocking review outcomes do not increment implementation no-progress counters.\n5. TestExecutionTrace_RecordsReviewAndRepairCycles verifies cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, review_group_id, reviewer_index, and final decision are appended for both initial and repair cycles.\n6. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run \"TestPreCloseReview|TestExecutionTrace\" -count=1 passes.\n7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-06T03:28:43.066338691Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-639af4ef","depends_on_id":"ddx-fb790086","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-06T03:28:43Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nThe pre-close review gate also needs bounded repair and terminal blocking policy. Without this slice, REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK can either loop forever or require manual handling even when a fixable implementation gap is clear.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:273 has OutcomeReason but no review classification taxonomy for repair cycles.\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576 suppresses no-progress for a narrow transient set and does not yet model review_error / operator-blocking classifications.\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:780 classifies parse errors but does not tie review errors to per-result/per-slot retry caps in loop state.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Classify non-approve review outcomes as review_fixable_gap, review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, or review_error.\n- For review_fixable_gap, schedule one bounded repair cycle carrying repair_context_from_review_group.\n- For terminal classes and exhausted review_error retries, block with needs_human/review-manual-required and do not consume no-progress budget.\n- Record append-only cycle trace for initial and repair cycles.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Initial review-group dispatch primitives.\n- Basic pre-close close/prevent-close integration.","id":"ddx-639af4ef","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","story:18","area:agent","kind:feature","reliability","adr:024","from:ddx-c851c3dd"],"parent":"ddx-42b917fe","priority":1,"spec_id":"ADR-024","status":"open","title":"review: add repair-cycle and terminal blocking policy for pre-close review","updated_at":"2026-05-06T03:28:43.673876787Z"}

Evidence covers the new `no_changes` result status, repeated one-shot queue runs reselecting the same bead, and the need for queue-drain behavior that either closes already-satisfied work or suppresses immediate retry on unresolved no-op attempts.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\nImplement no-change adjudication and retry suppression for execute-loop.\n\n## Goals\n- Run the defined satisfaction check after a `no_changes` execute-bead result\n- Auto-close beads only when the satisfaction check says the bead is already satisfied\n- Leave unresolved beads open but record retry suppression / cooldown so the next queue pass can move on\n- Persist enough evidence and state for operator visibility and deterministic tests","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"success\nresult_rev=720eef45799ffa90a4104e73e1fc5377363de066\nbase_rev=9e07dc4575b1edcda1270e5296794e0bb4b953b7","created_at":"2026-04-14T04:15:36.143762538Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"success"}],"execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T04:15:14.023886812Z","id":"ddx-640c1ece","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:bead","area:cli"],"owner":"ddx","parent":"ddx-d2930ee4","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-720eef4","spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Implement execute-loop no-change adjudication and retry suppression","updated_at":"2026-04-14T04:25:22.109965142Z"}

On hit it returns\n       `RunRequeueResult{Bead: \u003cexisting\u003e, Deduplicated: true}` with\n       no second reopen and no second audit event.\n     - Covered by TestRunRequeue_DuplicateKeyReturnsExisting\n       (resolver_mutation_runs_test.go:118).\n\n  4. Audit event on the originating bead\n     - resolver_mutation_runs.go:82-106 appends a bead.BeadEvent of\n       kind `run_requeue` (constant RunRequeueEventKind) to the\n       originating bead, with body recording operator identity, run\n       id, idempotency key, and the optional layer override.\n     - Covered by TestRunRequeue_ReopensBeadAndAuditLogs\n       (resolver_mutation_runs_test.go:58), which asserts the event\n       kind, body fields, and that the bead status is reopened.\n\n  5. Tests cover concurrent re-queue with same key\n     - TestRunRequeue_ConcurrentSameKey\n       (resolver_mutation_runs_test.go:180) fires N concurrent\n       RunRequeue calls with the same idempotencyKey and asserts\n       exactly one reopen + one audit event, with all other callers\n       receiving Deduplicated=true and the same bead.\n\nThe verification command below runs all four RunRequeue tests, which\ntogether exercise every AC item. Repro fixture bead-closed-no-evidence.jsonl (from ddx-004eccbc) replays through the execute-loop close path and is rejected — the bead does not transition to closed.\n3. Unit test covers the three terminal failure modes: reviewer exit non-zero, reviewer output empty, reviewer output unparseable.\n4. Most damaging of the three report findings because it hides incomplete work.\n\nObserved on axon `axon-c5cc071a` (RBAC field/row/entity spec, ~2000-word AC) 2026-04-19:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"id\": \"axon-c5cc071a\",\n  \"claimed-at\": \"2026-04-19T23:20:45Z\",\n  \"claimed-machine\": \"sindri\",\n  \"claimed-pid\": \"3479503\",\n  \"execute-loop-heartbeat-at\": \"2026-04-19T23:20:45.364638776Z\",\n  \"updated_at\": \"2026-04-19T23:25:18.98959532Z\",\n  \"status\": \"closed\",\n  \"events\": []\n}\n```\n\n4m33s from claim to closed, `events: []`, no execution bundle under `.ddx/executions/` referencing the bead, no git commit referencing the id, no branch, no notes. Reopened 2026-04-20 as `axon-c5cc071a`. Evidence this is worker-side: no other actor claimed the bead, `claimed-pid` matches execute-loop PID pattern, `execute-loop-heartbeat-at` was set at claim — a manual `ddx bead update` would not leave `events: []` while setting `status: closed`.\n\nRoot-cause hypothesis from report: the worker's cleanup path closes the bead on primary-task success even when the reviewer sub-step errors out without returning a parseable verdict (e.g. context-overflow `n_keep: 1,868,459 \u003e= n_ctx: 196,608` seen in reporter's LLM server logs, or any exception in the review state-machine path). A `bead.result` event that never gets emitted still leaves the bead closed if the exit path is not gated on verdict presence.\n\nClosure contract should be: a bead transitions to `status=closed` only when a valid terminal review verdict (APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK / SKIP) is recorded alongside an execution bundle and at least one bead event. Audit current code: any field in cobra/server/spec/runWorker NOT in this list = bug, must add.\n\n2. SEPARATE DispatchOptions struct holds control-plane flags (--local, --json) that must NOT appear on ExecuteLoopSpec or be persisted to spec.json. parseExecuteLoopSpec returns (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) — single cobra→spec conversion; called only from cobra RunE; never re-implemented.\n\n3. Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() exist as methods. Lifecycle:\n   (a) parseExecuteLoopSpec → cobra defaults applied via cobra binding\n   (b) client-side: Validate() before HTTP submit\n   (c) server-side: decode → ApplyDefaults() → Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop\n   (d) GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through the SAME StartExecuteLoop (no parallel construction)\n   (e) spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (ApplyDefaults already ran)\n\n4. JSON wire format for time.Duration uses string representation (e.g. \"30s\", \"5m\"). UnmarshalJSON tolerates both string and numeric (nanoseconds) for backward-compat. Custom Duration type or json.RawMessage→time.ParseDuration acceptable.\n\n5. cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go execute-loop / work RunE: constructs (spec, dispatch) via parseExecuteLoopSpec; dispatches: dispatch.Local → runExecuteLoopInline(spec); else httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec). NO positional-param flag passing remains.\n\n6. httpSubmitExecuteLoop marshals the spec directly as the HTTP request body (json.Marshal(spec)). No intermediate map[string]interface{}.\n\n7. Server handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes directly INTO ExecuteLoopSpec (no parallel request struct). The previously hand-maintained struct in server.go:2246-2260 is DELETED. ApplyDefaults + Validate run before StartExecuteLoop.\n\n8. GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter (cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137) DELETES its parallel request struct and parallel StartExecuteLoop literal. Goes through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop path as the REST handler.\n\n9. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec in workers.go is REPLACED by (or aliased to) ExecuteLoopSpec. spec.json on disk uses the unified struct.\n\n10. runWorker(spec) consumes spec.MaxCostUSD (with default fallback when zero), spec.RequestTimeout, spec.MinPower, spec.MaxPower, spec.OpaquePassthrough, spec.FromRev — no hardcoded escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD anywhere except the fallback case.\n\n11. Reflection-backed round-trip test (TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection): uses reflect over ExecuteLoopSpec's exported fields; for each, sets a non-zero/non-default value, marshals, unmarshals on the server side, asserts the value survives into spec.json AND into the runWorker-visible spec. Adding a future field = automatic test coverage (no row-by-row maintenance). Test FAILS if any exported field is missing source/sink coverage.\n\n12. Specific OpaquePassthrough assertion: server-submitted ddx work --harness claude persists opaque_passthrough:true and skips BOTH validation gates (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773). .ddx/executions/20260504T022245-36a2ca7b/prompt.md:742:+          "body": "Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits. ## Summary The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector. These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either: (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned. The bead's \"SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS\" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make. ## Evidence 1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims). 2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: \"Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands.\" Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`. 3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites). 4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (\"ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server\") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. ADR-022 step 7 requires migrating from in-process agent execution to a server-spawn model (workers spawned by the server, not by the CLI directly). The current execute-loop launch path uses cli/internal/server/workers.go handleStartExecuteLoopWorker to start a worker subprocess, but this handler may not properly validate all execution parameters or follow the unified ExecuteLoopSpec lifecycle (ApplyDefaults → Validate → StartExecuteLoop).

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/workers.go handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (exact line to be confirmed after ddx-89a9c305 lands): the current server-spawn path constructs CLIOverrides and ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime manually rather than going through ApplyDefaults → Validate → StartExecuteLoop with a unified spec.
- triage:needs-investigation label was added because the exact wiring was unclear at filing time. Investigation findings: the server-spawn handler calls StartExecuteLoop via the workers subsystem, but intermediate struct construction (before ddx-89a9c305 unifies the spec) may drop fields.
- The server binary is the one that actually execs 'ddx work'; the subprocess inherits from server-resolved settings, not from a fully validated ExecuteLoopSpec.

PROPOSED FIX
- Confirm handleStartExecuteLoopWorker in cli/internal/server/workers.go validates through ApplyDefaults → Validate before calling StartExecuteLoop.
- If gaps exist: wire the unified ExecuteLoopSpec (from ddx-89a9c305) into the spawn path.
- Verify the spawned 'ddx work' subprocess receives all spec fields (MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, OpaquePassthrough, FromRev) via the exec args or a spec file.

NON-SCOPE
- Changes to the exec'd ddx work subprocess behavior (that's C10 / ddx-f9acb86d).
- REST/GraphQL request decoding (those are ddx-da9e9491 and ddx-ce1d6309).

4. TestServerSpawnWorker_HonorsAllSpecFields verifies all ExecuteLoopSpec fields reach the subprocess.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

.ddx/executions/20260504T021759-c712d0e8/prompt.md:8:1. Single struct ExecuteLoopSpec in cli/internal/agent/ (or sub-package) with JSON tags. Includes ALL fields currently distributed across cobra extracts, server request struct, ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec, and runWorker consumption: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Once, PollInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, plus SpecVersion int. Audit current code: any field in cobra/server/spec/runWorker NOT in this list = bug, must add.

2. SEPARATE DispatchOptions struct holds control-plane flags (--local, --json) that must NOT appear on ExecuteLoopSpec or be persisted to spec.json. parseExecuteLoopSpec returns (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) — single cobra→spec conversion; called only from cobra RunE; never re-implemented.

3. Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() exist as methods. Lifecycle:
   (a) parseExecuteLoopSpec → cobra defaults applied via cobra binding
   (b) client-side: Validate() before HTTP submit
   (c) server-side: decode → ApplyDefaults() → Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop
   (d) GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through the SAME StartExecuteLoop (no parallel construction)
   (e) spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (ApplyDefaults already ran)

4. JSON wire format for time.Duration uses string representation (e.g. "30s", "5m"). UnmarshalJSON tolerates both string and numeric (nanoseconds) for backward-compat. Custom Duration type or json.RawMessage→time.ParseDuration acceptable.

5. cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go execute-loop / work RunE: constructs (spec, dispatch) via parseExecuteLoopSpec; dispatches: dispatch.Local → runExecuteLoopInline(spec); else httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec). NO positional-param flag passing remains.

6. httpSubmitExecuteLoop marshals the spec directly as the HTTP request body (json.Marshal(spec)). No intermediate map[string]interface{}.

7. Server handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes directly INTO ExecuteLoopSpec (no parallel request struct). The previously hand-maintained struct in server.go:2246-2260 is DELETED. ApplyDefaults + Validate run before StartExecuteLoop.

8. GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter (cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137) DELETES its parallel request struct and parallel StartExecuteLoop literal. Goes through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop path as the REST handler.

9. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec in workers.go is REPLACED by (or aliased to) ExecuteLoopSpec. spec.json on disk uses the unified struct.

10. runWorker(spec) consumes spec.MaxCostUSD (with default fallback when zero), spec.RequestTimeout, spec.MinPower, spec.MaxPower, spec.OpaquePassthrough, spec.FromRev — no hardcoded escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD anywhere except the fallback case.

11. Reflection-backed round-trip test (TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection): uses reflect over ExecuteLoopSpec's exported fields; for each, sets a non-zero/non-default value, marshals, unmarshals on the server side, asserts the value survives into spec.json AND into the runWorker-visible spec. Adding a future field = automatic test coverage (no row-by-row maintenance). Test FAILS if any exported field is missing source/sink coverage.

12. Specific OpaquePassthrough assertion: server-submitted ddx work --harness claude persists opaque_passthrough:true and skips BOTH validation gates (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773). Test names both line ranges.

13. Backward compatibility: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ continue to load. Specifically: legacy fields min_tier, max_tier, and any other historical fields are tolerated and ignored (json.Decoder default behavior; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads). Spot-check: load .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json without error.

14. Forward compatibility: SpecVersion int field added. Server rejects with 400 + capability list when client SpecVersion > server-supported. Same client/server version always works. Document the stance in code comment.

15. Regression test: --local path is unchanged in behavior. Existing local-path tests pass.

16. Manual verification: `ddx work --harness claude` (no --local) on a small queue completes at least one bead end-to-end without harness-rejection error.

17. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.

- Recommend landing AFTER ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive). If landing in parallel, coordinate execute_bead_loop.go merges.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker) is independent — LabelFilter stays string.

SEPARATE DispatchOptions struct holds control-plane flags (--local, --json) that must NOT appear on ExecuteLoopSpec or be persisted to spec.json. parseExecuteLoopSpec returns (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) — single cobra→spec conversion; called only from cobra RunE; never re-implemented.\n\n3. Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() exist as methods. Lifecycle:\n   (a) parseExecuteLoopSpec → cobra defaults applied via cobra binding\n   (b) client-side: Validate() before HTTP submit\n   (c) server-side: decode → ApplyDefaults() → Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop\n   (d) GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through the SAME StartExecuteLoop (no parallel construction)\n   (e) spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (ApplyDefaults already ran)\n\n4. JSON wire format for time.Duration uses string representation (e.g. \"30s\", \"5m\"). UnmarshalJSON tolerates both string and numeric (nanoseconds) for backward-compat. Custom Duration type or json.RawMessage→time.ParseDuration acceptable.\n\n5. cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go execute-loop / work RunE: constructs (spec, dispatch) via parseExecuteLoopSpec; dispatches: dispatch.Local → runExecuteLoopInline(spec); else httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec). NO positional-param flag passing remains.\n\n6. httpSubmitExecuteLoop marshals the spec directly as the HTTP request body (json.Marshal(spec)). No intermediate map[string]interface{}.\n\n7. Server handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes directly INTO ExecuteLoopSpec (no parallel request struct). The previously hand-maintained struct in server.go:2246-2260 is DELETED. ApplyDefaults + Validate run before StartExecuteLoop.\n\n8. GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter (cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137) DELETES its parallel request struct and parallel StartExecuteLoop literal. Goes through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop path as the REST handler.\n\n9. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec in workers.go is REPLACED by (or aliased to) ExecuteLoopSpec. spec.json on disk uses the unified struct.\n\n10. runWorker(spec) consumes spec.MaxCostUSD (with default fallback when zero), spec.RequestTimeout, spec.MinPower, spec.MaxPower, spec.OpaquePassthrough, spec.FromRev — no hardcoded escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD anywhere except the fallback case.\n\n11. Reflection-backed round-trip test (TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection): uses reflect over ExecuteLoopSpec's exported fields; for each, sets a non-zero/non-default value, marshals, unmarshals on the server side, asserts the value survives into spec.json AND into the runWorker-visible spec. Adding a future field = automatic test coverage (no row-by-row maintenance). Test FAILS if any exported field is missing source/sink coverage.\n\n12. Specific OpaquePassthrough assertion: server-submitted ddx work --harness claude persists opaque_passthrough:true and skips BOTH validation gates (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773). Test names both line ranges.\n\n13. Backward compatibility: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ continue to load. Specifically: legacy fields min_tier, max_tier, and any other historical fields are tolerated and ignored (json.Decoder default behavior; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads). Spot-check: load .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json without error.\n\n14. Forward compatibility: SpecVersion int field added. Server rejects with 400 + capability list when client SpecVersion \u003e server-supported. Same client/server version always works. Document the stance in code comment.\n\n15. Regression test: --local path is unchanged in behavior. Existing local-path tests pass.\n\n16. Manual verification: `ddx work --harness claude` (no --local) on a small queue completes at least one bead end-to-end without harness-rejection error.\n\n17. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.\n\nDEPENDENCIES\n- Recommend landing AFTER ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive). If landing in parallel, coordinate execute_bead_loop.go merges.\n- ddx-9d55601f (picker) is independent — LabelFilter stays string.\n- REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744): new package added as entry-root.",
.ddx/executions/20260504T021759-c712d0e8/manifest.json:591:          "body": "Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits. ## Summary The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector. These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either: (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned. The bead's \"SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS\" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make. ## Evidence 1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims). 2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: \"Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands.\" Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`. 3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites). 4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (\"ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server\") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec. 5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands. ## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict) The bead also fails the size check on its own: - 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run). - Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals. - File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably \u003e500 LOC across \u003e5 files in unrelated packages. - Per execution-bead protocol size check: \"More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems\" + \"More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages\" → DECOMPOSE. ## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human: Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct. Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one. Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded. Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick. ## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage 1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task. 2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead \"add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler\" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway). 3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate. 4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped. ## What this attempt did NOT do (and why) - No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice. - No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see \"Why I am not decomposing\" above. - No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's status.",

Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() exist as methods. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits.\n\n## Summary\n\nThe bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector.\n\nThese are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either:\n  (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or\n  (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned.\n\nThe bead's \"SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS\" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make.\n\n## Evidence\n\n1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims).\n2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: \"Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands.\" Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`.\n3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites).\n4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (\"ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server\") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec.\n5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands.\n\n## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict)\n\nThe bead also fails the size check on its own:\n\n- 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run).\n- Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals.\n- File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably \u003e500 LOC across \u003e5 files in unrelated packages.\n- Per execution-bead protocol size check: \"More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems\" + \"More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages\" → DECOMPOSE.\n\n## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt\n\nThe protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human:\n\n  Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct.\n  Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one.\n  Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded.\n\nEach option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick.\n\n## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage\n\n1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task.\n2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead \"add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler\" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway).\n3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate.\n4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped.\n\n## What this attempt did NOT do (and why)\n\n- No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice.\n- No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see \"Why I am not decomposing\" above.\n- No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. .ddx/executions/20260503T110921-b062acad/prompt.md:5:OBSERVED (operator report, 2026-05-03)
Bare `ddx work` (no --harness, no --model, no --provider, no --profile pins) repeatedly rejects with:
  "no viable routing candidate: 4 candidates rejected"
No reason logged for any of the 4 rejections. Same machine has codex working fine via direct invocation; codex has been running all day on niflheim. The rejections happen BEFORE the request reaches fizeau, so fizeau never gets a chance to pick a harness.

EXPECTED
Bare `ddx work` should pass through to fizeau with NO routing pins; fizeau should pick any available harness with quota. DDx should NOT pre-reject candidates based on its own (likely stale or incomplete) view of provider availability.

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESES
1. cli/internal/agent/runner.go ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService (or its caller at cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go around line 1647) is enforcing pre-flight provider availability checks against a stale or incorrect provider list.
2. cli/internal/escalation/InferTier or similar is computing a tier that has no candidate matches, treating that as fatal instead of passing through to fizeau's auto-routing.
3. cli/internal/agent/service_run.go is constructing ServiceExecuteRequest with constraints that exclude all 4 known harnesses despite operator passing no pins.
4. The 4 candidates being rejected may be the 4 harnesses (claude/codex/agent/embedded); reasons could include: stale RouteStatus from fizeau, missing capability declaration, provider-health classification, cost-cap pre-check.
5. Related to ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute gap with explicit harness pin) but distinct — that's about filling Profile/MinPower; this is about DDx rejecting before fizeau gets called at all.

INVESTIGATION REQUIRED
- Capture the actual rejection reasons for each of the 4 candidates (add structured logging or read existing route_attempt events from the bead's event stream).
- Compare DDx's view of harness availability vs fizeau's `svc.ListHarnesses` / `svc.RouteStatus`.
- Verify that bare `ddx work` (no pins, no opaque-passthrough) actually invokes fizeau's auto-routing and not DDx's local pre-filter.

FIX SHAPE
- DDx pre-flight should refuse to filter candidates that fizeau hasn't explicitly excluded. If DDx knows nothing pinned, the request goes through to fizeau as `ServiceExecuteRequest{Harness:"", Model:"", Profile:"<inferred-tier>"}` and fizeau picks.
- "No viable routing candidate" error should ONLY come from fizeau, with fizeau's reasons surfaced. DDx should not synthesize its own rejection.
- If DDx has any pre-filter today, log per-candidate reasons so operator can see why each was rejected.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fizeau-side routing improvements (separate bead ddx-1e516bc9 if needed)
- Reshaping the cobra flag surface
- Changing default tier inference

INTERSECTION
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): if the unification covers the per-attempt RouteRequest construction, this fix might naturally fold in.
- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute gap): if fizeau auto-routes within explicit harness, that fix complements this. They're distinct: this bead is about DDx not over-filtering BEFORE fizeau sees the request.
- ddx-848069a3 C8 (routing preflight to Drain startup): if the preflight relocates as part of the refactor, the fix should land in the new location.

.ddx/executions/20260503T021424-dac100b6/prompt.md:5:Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

Proposed architectural shift: WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API.

DESIGN PROPOSALS (this TD chooses among them)

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

INTEGRATION WITH IN-FLIGHT WORK

This TD intersects:
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): the spec becomes the registration payload — single struct still applies
- ddx-5cb6e6cd refactor epic: the work package boundary aligns with worker autonomy; C9 (StopCondition) becomes worker-side state
- ddx-4c51d33e (cross-project leak): workers identify their project at registration; server enforces project boundaries via session token
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): becomes natural — worker stays alive, polls for work, doesn't exit on empty queue

IMPLEMENTATION OUTLINE (post-TD)
1. Define API contract + handlers (cli/internal/server/worker_api.go)
2. Add worker client (cli/internal/agent/work/client.go) implementing the API
3. Add in-process API implementation for --local mode (cli/internal/agent/work/local_api.go)
4. Migrate execute_bead_loop / drain_loop to consume the API instead of direct store access
5. Migrate server worker spawn to register-then-poll instead of inline-execution

NOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS TD
- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads)
- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)
- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)

OUTPUTS (TD deliverables)
- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- Updated FEAT-006 (or new FEAT) describing the worker contract
- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown
- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly
- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups

DEPS
- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing
- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads

.ddx/executions/20260503T021724-8a33226e/prompt.md:5:The current cooldown logic treats any ExecutionFailed status as evidence the model can't make progress, applying noProgressCooldown=6h when BaseRev == ResultRev (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1074-1086 via shouldSuppressNoProgress at line 1550). This silently freezes important work whenever a worker is disrupted (killed, server restart, network blip, OS issue) — disruption produces ExecutionFailed + no commit, indistinguishable from "model genuinely tried and produced nothing."

OBSERVED (today, 2026-05-02)
After the picker fix investigation found the four "starved" P0 beads (ddx-dc157075, ddx-aee651ec, ddx-29058e2a, ddx-9d55601f) all had execute-loop-retry-after timestamps ~3 hours in the future. Investigation per ddx-9d55601f confirmed these were NOT genuine model failures — they were prior worker disruptions during this development session (worker kills, server restarts) being treated as model-gave-up.

User directive: "Cooldown should be reserved for cases where the model is truly unable to make progress on a task."

CURRENT COOLDOWN POLICY (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)
- LandConflictCooldown = 15min — short cooldown after merge/land conflicts (line 64)
- noProgressCooldown = 6h default — when shouldSuppressNoProgress is true (line 367)
- MaxLoopCooldown = 24h — push_failed, push_conflict, decomposition decline (line 57)

shouldSuppressNoProgress (line 1550): returns true if BaseRev != "" AND ResultRev != "" AND BaseRev == ResultRev. This catches:
- Model wrote nothing AND came back cleanly (true no_progress) ✓
- Worker killed mid-execution before commit (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Network/provider error before model started (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Routing preflight rejection (FALSE no_progress — config issue)

The mis-classification is the bug.

PROPOSED FIX

Add a Disposition::Disrupted variant (or equivalent flag on ExecuteBeadReport) that explicitly marks results that are NOT evidence of model inability:
- ctx.Err() != nil (cancellation, deadline, parent died)
- syscall.SIGTERM / SIGINT received during execution
- Provider-side 5xx, connection refused, timeout (transport disruption)
- Worker process killed (parent never received clean result)
- Server-restart-detected marker
- Routing preflight rejection (operator config issue, not model failure)

For Disrupted: skip cooldown entirely (or use a tiny defer like 30s) and unclaim the bead so the next worker pickup is immediate.
For genuine no-progress (model returned clean with no commit AND rationale present): keep current 6h cooldown.

ALTERNATIVE: exponential backoff per bead. First failure: 5min; second: 30min; third: 2h; cap at 24h. Resets on any successful attempt or operator clear. This is more forgiving than the current flat 6h on the first hiccup.

PROBABLY BOTH: Disrupted skips cooldown entirely; non-Disrupted failures use exponential backoff starting at 5min, not flat 6h.

DETECTION POINTERS

- ctx.Err() check at the call site that produces ExecutionFailed (line 513, 629, 637, 1011)
- Check for `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt` presence — if missing, the model never wrote one (likely disruption)
- Check the executor's exit code: SIGKILL/SIGTERM = disruption; clean exit with empty result = no-progress
- Track parent process restart count or boot ID; if changed since the attempt started, mark Disrupted

NOT IN SCOPE
- Changing the cooldown duration constants themselves
- Reworking the per-bead retry policy framework (separate concern)
- Changing land-conflict or push-failed cooldowns (those are correct as-is)
- The exponential-backoff alternative is OPTIONAL; primary fix is Disrupted classification

INTERSECTION WITH OTHER BEADS
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): the stay-alive fix prevents some classes of disruption (worker no longer exits on empty poll), but does NOT fix the cooldown-on-disruption mis-classification
- ddx-076147ee (worker client-server architecture TD): if workers become long-lived clients, server-restart disruption goes away by construction. But this fix is needed BEFORE that TD is implemented because the disruption classification is needed regardless.
- ddx-9228a484 C9 (StopCondition enum): may absorb this — the StopCondition enum could include Disrupted as a state. Verify and align.

2. Detection at: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go where ExecutionFailed is set (current lines 513 routing-preflight, 629/637 executor-error, 1011 in execute_bead.go) — each set point evaluates whether the failure is Disrupted.
3. The cooldown branch (lines 1074-1086) checks Disrupted FIRST: if Disrupted, skip SetExecutionCooldown entirely; just Unclaim so the bead returns to ready.
4. shouldSuppressNoProgress unchanged for genuine no-progress (BaseRev == ResultRev AND not Disrupted AND no rationale).
5. New diagnostic event `disruption_detected` with kind, reason, and bead context — emitted when Disrupted is set so operators can see disruption rates.
6. (Optional) Exponential backoff for non-Disrupted no-progress: first failure 5min; second 30min; third 2h; cap at 24h. Reset on success. If implementing: replace flat noProgressCooldown with this curve; document in code comment.
7. Tests:
   - TestLoop_DisruptedExecution_NoCooldown — context cancelled mid-execution; assert no execute-loop-retry-after set; bead returns to ready
   - TestLoop_GenuineNoProgress_StillCooldowns — model returns clean with no commit + rationale; assert 6h cooldown set
   - TestLoop_PreflightRejection_NoCooldown — routing preflight fails; assert disruption classification (operator config, not model failure)
   - TestLoop_DisruptionEventEmitted — assert disruption_detected event appears in bead events
8. Manual verification: kill an in-flight worker mid-execution; assert the bead is immediately re-claimable by the next worker (no 6h park).
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Documentation: update FEAT-006 (or wherever the cooldown policy is documented) to describe Disrupted vs no-progress and the policy rationale.

The rejections happen BEFORE the request reaches fizeau, so fizeau never gets a chance to pick a harness.\n\nEXPECTED\nBare `ddx work` should pass through to fizeau with NO routing pins; fizeau should pick any available harness with quota. DDx should NOT pre-reject candidates based on its own (likely stale or incomplete) view of provider availability.\n\nROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESES\n1. cli/internal/agent/runner.go ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService (or its caller at cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go around line 1647) is enforcing pre-flight provider availability checks against a stale or incorrect provider list.\n2. cli/internal/escalation/InferTier or similar is computing a tier that has no candidate matches, treating that as fatal instead of passing through to fizeau's auto-routing.\n3. cli/internal/agent/service_run.go is constructing ServiceExecuteRequest with constraints that exclude all 4 known harnesses despite operator passing no pins.\n4. The 4 candidates being rejected may be the 4 harnesses (claude/codex/agent/embedded); reasons could include: stale RouteStatus from fizeau, missing capability declaration, provider-health classification, cost-cap pre-check.\n5. .ddx/executions/20260503T030841-e004715a/prompt.md:5:OBSERVED (operator report, 2026-05-03)
Bare `ddx work` (no --harness, no --model, no --provider, no --profile pins) repeatedly rejects with:
  "no viable routing candidate: 4 candidates rejected"
No reason logged for any of the 4 rejections. Same machine has codex working fine via direct invocation; codex has been running all day on niflheim. The rejections happen BEFORE the request reaches fizeau, so fizeau never gets a chance to pick a harness.

EXPECTED
Bare `ddx work` should pass through to fizeau with NO routing pins; fizeau should pick any available harness with quota. DDx should NOT pre-reject candidates based on its own (likely stale or incomplete) view of provider availability.

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESES
1. cli/internal/agent/runner.go ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService (or its caller at cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go around line 1647) is enforcing pre-flight provider availability checks against a stale or incorrect provider list.
2. cli/internal/escalation/InferTier or similar is computing a tier that has no candidate matches, treating that as fatal instead of passing through to fizeau's auto-routing.
3. cli/internal/agent/service_run.go is constructing ServiceExecuteRequest with constraints that exclude all 4 known harnesses despite operator passing no pins.
4. The 4 candidates being rejected may be the 4 harnesses (claude/codex/agent/embedded); reasons could include: stale RouteStatus from fizeau, missing capability declaration, provider-health classification, cost-cap pre-check.
5. Related to ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute gap with explicit harness pin) but distinct — that's about filling Profile/MinPower; this is about DDx rejecting before fizeau gets called at all.

INVESTIGATION REQUIRED
- Capture the actual rejection reasons for each of the 4 candidates (add structured logging or read existing route_attempt events from the bead's event stream).
- Compare DDx's view of harness availability vs fizeau's `svc.ListHarnesses` / `svc.RouteStatus`.
- Verify that bare `ddx work` (no pins, no opaque-passthrough) actually invokes fizeau's auto-routing and not DDx's local pre-filter.

FIX SHAPE
- DDx pre-flight should refuse to filter candidates that fizeau hasn't explicitly excluded. If DDx knows nothing pinned, the request goes through to fizeau as `ServiceExecuteRequest{Harness:"", Model:"", Profile:"<inferred-tier>"}` and fizeau picks.
- "No viable routing candidate" error should ONLY come from fizeau, with fizeau's reasons surfaced. DDx should not synthesize its own rejection.
- If DDx has any pre-filter today, log per-candidate reasons so operator can see why each was rejected.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fizeau-side routing improvements (separate bead ddx-1e516bc9 if needed)
- Reshaping the cobra flag surface
- Changing default tier inference

INTERSECTION
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): if the unification covers the per-attempt RouteRequest construction, this fix might naturally fold in.
- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute gap): if fizeau auto-routes within explicit harness, that fix complements this. They're distinct: this bead is about DDx not over-filtering BEFORE fizeau sees the request.
- ddx-848069a3 C8 (routing preflight to Drain startup): if the preflight relocates as part of the refactor, the fix should land in the new location.

.ddx/executions/20260502T205432-6a763ee3/prompt.md:5:ddx work / ddx agent execute-loop has two execution paths: --local (inline) and default (submit to running server). The server path silently drops five CLI flags between client → server request → ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec → spawned worker subprocess. Most user-visible symptom: `ddx work --harness claude` fails when going through the server but works under --local, because the server validates the harness against its provider list instead of treating it as an opaque passthrough constraint.

USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM
- `ddx work --harness claude --local` works.
- `ddx work --harness claude` (server path) fails.
- Server path is supposed to be a transparent passthrough — same flags, same behavior.

ROOT CAUSE: chain of omissions

1. CLIENT — cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1608-1611 extracts maxCostUSD, requestTimeout, minPower, maxPower from the cobra command but cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627 (call to executeLoopWithServer) and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1941 (the function signature) do NOT include them. The four flags are dropped client-side before any HTTP call.

2. CLIENT → SERVER — opaque_passthrough IS sent in the worker spec at cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1948-1950, BUT:

3. SERVER REQUEST STRUCT — cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260 (handleStartExecuteLoopWorker) request struct is MISSING fields for OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower. Server JSON-decode silently drops the unknown fields.

4. SERVER → SPEC — cli/internal/server/server.go:2289-2303 (call to StartExecuteLoop) does not set OpaquePassthrough on the spec (since it never decoded it). MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower aren't even spec fields yet.

5. WORKER SPAWN — cli/internal/server/workers.go:656 hardcodes escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD instead of spec.MaxCostUSD. RequestTimeout override never applied.

6. VALIDATION DIVERGENCE — at cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1647-1652, --local correctly skips harness validation when treatPassthroughAsOpaque=true (the ddx work case). On the server path, the same skip never happens because OpaquePassthrough was lost in step 3 — so harness 'claude' goes through ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService which rejects it. **This is the user's observed bug.**

WHY THIS IS A REAL BUG (not "just configure the server")
The server should be a transparent passthrough — every CLI flag accepted by ddx work should produce identical behavior whether routed --local or via the server. Silently dropping flags violates the contract and produces confusing failures (the local path works, the server path doesn't, with no error explaining why).

FIX SHAPE
- Add fields to server request struct (server.go:2246-2260): OpaquePassthrough bool, MaxCostUSD float64, RequestTimeout string, MinPower int, MaxPower int
- Add parameters to executeLoopWithServer signature (agent_cmd.go:1941); pass maxCostUSD/requestTimeout/minPower/maxPower from caller (agent_cmd.go:1627)
- Add fields to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec where missing (workers.go:25-49) — MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower
- Populate spec from request in handler (server.go:2289-2303)
- Use spec values in runWorker (workers.go:656 cost cap; ~594-601 request-timeout override)

NOT IN SCOPE
- Reworking the client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is; just add fields).
- Changing the validation logic itself — only ensuring opaque_passthrough actually gates validation as intended.
.ddx/executions/20260502T205432-6a763ee3/prompt.md:8:1. cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: executeLoopWithServer signature extended to accept maxCostUSD, requestTimeout, minPower, maxPower; caller at line ~1627 passes them; opaque_passthrough already passed (verify present).
2. cli/internal/server/server.go: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker request struct decodes OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower; StartExecuteLoop call populates the spec with all five.
3. cli/internal/server/workers.go: ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec gains MaxCostUSD float64, RequestTimeout string, MinPower int, MaxPower int (OpaquePassthrough already exists). runWorker uses spec.MaxCostUSD (fallback to DefaultMaxCostUSD if zero) and applies spec.RequestTimeout / MinPower / MaxPower the same way the local path does.
4. The harness-validation skip at agent_cmd.go:1647-1652 is reachable on the server path: if OpaquePassthrough is true on the spec, the server-spawned worker subprocess receives --opaque-passthrough (or equivalent state) so its own preflight validation skips the harness check. End-to-end: `ddx work --harness claude` succeeds via the server path.
5. Table-driven test (workers_test.go or new file): for each of the 5 flags, assert the value submitted to the server appears unchanged in the persisted .ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json AND in the spawned worker subprocess argv. Use a fake spawner or argv-capture seam.
6. End-to-end test: spawn a worker via the server endpoint with --harness=claude, --max-cost=99, --request-timeout=42m, --min-power=2, --max-power=7. Assert all five values land in the worker spec on disk.
7. Manual verification: `ddx work --harness claude` (no --local) on a small queue completes at least one bead end-to-end without harness-rejection error.
8. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/server/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
9. Regression test: confirm --local path is unchanged (existing tests still pass).

- no_changes / execution_failed triage paths (this child is review-only).

.ddx/executions/20260430T181254-ac5738cc/prompt.md:5:## Context

Follow-up observability/catalog work after the MinPower/MaxPower execution boundary in ddx-c4231775.

Upstream agent exposes available models with power numbers and may expose route candidate traces for debug. DDx status surfaces may consume model/power data for operator visibility. DDx may consume ResolveRoute/Candidates only on operator status/debug surfaces. Normal run/try/work execution must not call ResolveRoute and must not record preflight candidate traces as execution evidence.

## Scope

1. Status CLI/UI: render available agent models and their power numbers.
2. Optional route-status debug: render RouteDecision.Candidates for display only.
3. Tests: model/power catalog rendering and no-viable-candidate trace rendering where route-status remains.

## Out of scope

- No retry threshold helper; ddx-67ed1062 owns MinPower threshold math.
- No run/try/work preflight gate.
- No execution-path ResolveRoute calls.
- No bead kind:routing evidence populated from RouteDecision.Candidates.
- No use of candidate traces or catalog rows to select or override harness/provider/model.

Execution evidence should record requested MinPower/MaxPower, actual model/power, effort/task facts, and cost/session metadata returned by Execute.

.ddx/executions/20260430T163724-3dfb1c46/prompt.md:5:Review finding amended by passthrough and upstream MinPower/MaxPower requirement.
File: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:373
Category: correctness
Severity: critical
Description: DDx calls agent.ResolveRoute in the execution path, copies the informational RouteDecision into Harness/Provider/Model overrides, and then calls Execute. This violates the boundary. Under the updated interface, DDx may request MinPower/MaxPower bounds and may pass harness/provider/model operator constraints through unchanged, but must not pre-resolve or re-inject a concrete route.
Suggested fix: Remove ResolveRoute from run/try/work execution paths. Pass only caller intent/task facts to Execute: MinPower/MaxPower, opaque AgentPassthrough constraints, Reasoning/Effort, Permissions, EstimatedPromptTokens, RequiresTools, workdir/timeouts/metadata. Keep ResolveRoute only in status/debug surfaces.

File: cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:159
Category: correctness
Severity: critical
Description: executeOnService currently does not carry DDx-owned execution intent correctly. With the updated agent interface, DDx must pass MinPower/MaxPower and opaque passthrough constraints to Execute, then record the agent's actual model/power response. Without that, DDx cannot own bead retry escalation or audit operator constraints.
Suggested fix: Populate ServiceExecuteRequest.MinPower/MaxPower and Harness/Provider/Model from the narrow passthrough envelope, capture actual model/power from Execute response/events, and persist requested_min_power/requested_max_power, actual model/power, plus requested passthrough in run and attempt evidence.
